Vistas · Digital AMC

An agentic paper-trading firm on the terminal — 11 sector analysts · 20 fund managers across 5 divisions · 1 CIO
PAPER · 2026-05
data as of 2026-05
CIO — firm conclusion
BOTTOM LINE: trade the gaps between the lenses, harvest breadth, refuse crowds and lone-ARM heroics. TOP ACTIONABLES: (1) VEDL -- firm-wide hold-underweight, do-not-add across all desks until flow reverses; flow (-13,051cr) is the leading signal over ARM 70. (2) LT -- sanctioned active underweight to mandate (Q4 fade), capped by each desk's TE budget, deepest in thematic Infra. (3) BHARTIARTL -- hold CORE on +Rs8,521cr flow + rising ARM + FII; NO overweight in concentrated/Focused books until the optical-PAT delta is confirmed. (4) LICI -- wait for flow; benchmark-weight everywhere except a tiny starter in the Value contra book. (5) Cap cross-desk SOLAR concentration (Premier/Waaree/Solar Inds) at the firm level; Mid Cap correctly declines, Infra holds capped. (6) OVERWEIGHT the Core Equity breadth engine (Multicap/Flexi) and the Healthcare confirm-breadth (Pharma desk); express LT/WIPRO/VEDL as the firm's coordinated underweights. Every overweight must be a triple-confirmed Q1 name sized as breadth -- the portfolio is the edge, no single name is.

Digital firms & schemes per-AMC paper-trading firms — pick a firm, then click a scheme for its scorecard, fact sheet & blotter
A digital firm = one paper book per distinct equity/hybrid scheme of that AMC, each run by the deterministic rules-FM under its own SEBI mandate + liquidity caps (paper-money, no look-ahead, free — no LLM). Firm AUM = Σ each scheme's real AUM. Beat bench · paper = desks whose paper-track CAGR exceeds their benchmark TR since 2015.
★ Read the historical track honestly: the 2015→ replay selects from the broad point-in-time market under each scheme's mandate caps + FM brain — it is NOT restricted to a thematic scheme's sector constituents. So a SECTOR/THEME fund's historical CAGR/IR reflects the rules-FM's broad-universe book (which is why several thematic desks converge to similar numbers), not the theme itself; the diversified mandates (Large/Flexi/Value/Multi-Cap/Small…) ARE properly differentiated. The current fact sheet / holdings (the seam book) IS each scheme's real disclosed portfolio — theme-faithful. A theme-restricted historical replay is a known follow-up.

—
book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.20 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +1.53% excess CAGR per 7.6% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.77 — about 76% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.47 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +7.63% excess CAGR per 16.3% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.39 — about 39% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Banking and Financial Services Fund - Direct Plan - Growth — the paper book ran 6.66% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.59 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.69% excess CAGR per 16.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.

IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.59 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.70% excess CAGR per 16.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.45 — about 45% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Conglomerate Fund-Direct Growth — the paper book ran 17.52% behind the actual fund over 1.5 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

NAV vs benchmark paper-trade book NAV overlaid on its benchmark TR, rebased to 100 at inception
— Book NAV 619   - - NIFTY 500 366 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years
Book CAGR
17.35%
vol 24.7% · Sharpe 0.78 · MaxDD -44%
vs NIFTY 500
+5.31%
excess CAGR over benchmark TR
Information Ratio
+0.33
TE 16.0% · β 1.17
IC (skill)
+0.044
t-stat 4.7 · per-bet forecast↔outcome corr
Transfer coeff.
0.38
how much skill survives the long-only / cap constraints
vs REAL scheme
+3.76%
book − real CAGR · real 13.6% over 11.4y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.33 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +5.31% excess CAGR per 16.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.38 — about 38% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Consumption Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 3.76% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.43 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +14.81% excess CAGR per 10.3% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.034 (t 6.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.45 — about 44% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.60 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +8.08% excess CAGR per 13.5% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.042 (t 4.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.38 — about 38% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 9.63% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.57 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +4.28% excess CAGR per 7.5% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.77 — about 77% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR -0.39 — the book lags its benchmark: it earned -4.02% excess CAGR per 10.2% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.76 — about 76% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL ESG Integration Strategy Fund (G)

— Book NAV 956   - - NIFTY 500 366 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years
Book CAGR
21.90%
vol 24.1% · Sharpe 0.96 · MaxDD -49%
vs NIFTY 500
+9.87%
excess CAGR over benchmark TR
Information Ratio
+0.62
TE 15.8% · β 1.13
IC (skill)
+0.044
t-stat 4.7 · per-bet forecast↔outcome corr
Transfer coeff.
0.54
how much skill survives the long-only / cap constraints
vs REAL scheme
+11.58%
book − real CAGR · real 12.4% over 5.5y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.62 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.87% excess CAGR per 15.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.54 — about 54% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life ESG Integration Strategy Fund-Direct Plan-Growth — the paper book ran 11.58% ahead of the actual fund over 5.5 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.53 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +6.59% excess CAGR per 12.5% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.042 (t 4.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.38 — about 38% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Flexi Cap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 5.08% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL Focused Fund (G)

NAV vs benchmark paper-trade book NAV overlaid on its benchmark TR, rebased to 100 at inception
— Book NAV 755   - - NIFTY 500 366 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years
Book CAGR
19.40%
vol 24.0% · Sharpe 0.87 · MaxDD -43%
vs NIFTY 500
+7.37%
excess CAGR over benchmark TR
Information Ratio
+0.47
TE 15.8% · β 1.12
IC (skill)
+0.042
t-stat 4.9 · per-bet forecast↔outcome corr
Transfer coeff.
0.41
how much skill survives the long-only / cap constraints
vs REAL scheme
+7.46%
book − real CAGR · real 11.9% over 11.4y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.47 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +7.37% excess CAGR per 15.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.042 (t 4.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.41 — about 41% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Focused Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 7.46% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years

vol 21.4% · Sharpe 0.98 · MaxDD -41%

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.62 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +6.19% excess CAGR per 10.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 3.1) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.82 — about 82% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Large & Mid Cap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 8.53% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.28 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +2.80% excess CAGR per 10.1% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 2.4) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.89 — about 89% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Large & Mid Cap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 2.22% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

— Book NAV 951   - - NIFTY 500 366 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years
Book CAGR
21.84%
vol 24.6% · Sharpe 0.94 · MaxDD -46%
vs NIFTY 500
+9.81%
excess CAGR over benchmark TR
Information Ratio
+0.60
TE 16.2% · β 1.15
IC (skill)
+0.044
t-stat 4.7 · per-bet forecast↔outcome corr

vs REAL scheme

book − real CAGR · real 13.2% over 11.4y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.60 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.81% excess CAGR per 16.2% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.48 — about 48% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Manufacturing Equity Fund - Direct Plan - Growth — the paper book ran 8.59% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.46 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +3.68% excess CAGR per 8.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 2.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.87 — about 87% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Midcap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 5.43% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

— Book NAV 790   - - NIFTY 500 366 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years
Book CAGR
19.87%
vol 24.9% · Sharpe 0.87 · MaxDD -45%
vs NIFTY 500

excess CAGR over benchmark TR

+0.48
TE 16.3% · β 1.17

+0.044

vs REAL scheme
+11.09%
book − real CAGR · real 8.8% over 11.4y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.48 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +7.84% excess CAGR per 16.3% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.40 — about 40% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life MNC Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 11.09% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.21 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +1.62% excess CAGR per 7.7% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.76 — about 76% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Aditya Birla SL Multi-Cap Fund (G)

Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.77 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +10.49% excess CAGR per 13.7% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.042 (t 4.9) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.41 — about 40% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Multi-Cap Fund-Direct Growth — the paper book ran 7.25% ahead of the actual fund over 5.1 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years
Book CAGR
21.84%
vol 24.4% · Sharpe 0.95 · MaxDD -47%
vs NIFTY 500

excess CAGR over benchmark TR

+0.61
TE 16.1% · β 1.14

0.50
how much skill survives the long-only / cap constraints

book − real CAGR · real 21.1% over 7.0y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.61 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.81% excess CAGR per 16.1% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.50 — about 50% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Pharma and Healthcare Fund-Direct-Growth — the paper book ran 7.75% ahead of the actual fund over 7.0 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

vs REAL scheme

book − real CAGR · real 13.9% over 11.4y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +1.03 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +6.65% excess CAGR per 6.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.045 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.61 — about 61% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Small Cap Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 6.80% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

how much skill survives the long-only / cap constraints
vs REAL scheme
+6.51%
book − real CAGR · real 21.3% over 5.7y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.61 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.81% excess CAGR per 16.2% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.49 — about 49% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Special Opportunities Fund-Direct-Growth — the paper book ran 6.51% ahead of the actual fund over 5.7 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.60 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +9.78% excess CAGR per 16.4% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.044 (t 4.7) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.

IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC

  • vs real Aditya Birla Sun Life Value Fund - Growth - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 11.58% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

ICICI Pru Large Cap Fund (G)

— Book NAV 415   - - NIFTY 100 328 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.24 — the book edges its benchmark: it earned +2.32% excess CAGR per 9.8% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.037 (t 2.4) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.82 — about 82% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real ICICI Prudential Large Cap Fund (erstwhile Bluechip Fund) - Direct Plan - Growth — the paper book ran 0.67% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

— Book NAV 772   - - NIFTY SMALLCAP 250 449 · rebased to 100 at inception
Live-forward NAV the paper book's actual track since the live seam, marked daily — the agentic firm going forward
Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)
Scorecard paper-trade skill vs benchmark TR & the real fund
Window 2015-01-30 → 2026-06-25 · 11.4 years

Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.92 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +5.56% excess CAGR per 6.0% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.045 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.43 — about 43% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
  • vs real quant Small Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan — the paper book ran 0.50% ahead of the actual fund over 11.4 years.
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-forward paper track begins 2026-06-25 at NAV 100 and is marked every trading day forward — one observation so far. (The chart above is the 2015→ rules-FM replay; this track is the firm's actual go-forward performance.)

book − real CAGR · real — over —y
Plain-English read — Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC · √breadth · TC
  • IR +0.34 — the book adds value over its benchmark: it earned +2.43% excess CAGR per 7.1% of tracking error.
  • IC +0.047 (t 5.0) — the selection signal is genuinely informative; ~0.05 is a good single-name forecast correlation.
  • TC 0.61 — about 61% of the raw signal survives the long-only / position-cap constraints (the rest is the implementation leak).
IC = mean cross-sectional Spearman(ARM score, next-rebalance return) over the candidate universe; TC = mean corr(actual target weights, unconstrained score-weights); BR is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are NOT independent — true breadth is far lower), so implied_IR is an upper bound on what the signal could deliver, to be read against the realized IR.
Definitions. IC (information coefficient) = the rank correlation between the signal's forecast and the realised next-period return, per bet — how often the call is right. Breadth (BR) = the number of independent bets per year — more, de-correlated bets compound skill. TC (transfer coefficient) = the fraction of that skill that actually reaches the live portfolio after long-only and position-size constraints. IR (information ratio) = excess return ÷ tracking error — the realised skill the law predicts as IC·√BR·TC. Breadth here is an UPPER bound (monthly holdings are not independent), so implied IR is a ceiling, not a forecast.

Live-Forward — the LLM fund managers take the seat as of 2026-06-25 · from the seam on, the agents decide; a deterministic guardrail enforces the mandate
The historical track was built by the deterministic rules-FM. From the latest data date forward, the LLM fund-manager agents read their desk (the inherited book + a candidate universe with validated signals + the rules-FM target as a reference) and set a genuine conviction target book; a deterministic guardrail then clips to the mandate / liquidity / equity-floor. Every bet is pre-registered with a falsifier (anti-hindsight), it is paper-money only with no look-ahead, and the committed audit trail carries no licensed analyst values. Skill will be scored — once next month's data lands — by whether the pre-registered theses played out, via the Fundamental Law (IC·√breadth·TC).
CIO — firm review of this round
The house has converged this round on ONE macro bet: a cheap energy/materials/commodity cluster (ONGC, NMDC, COALINDIA, HINDALCO, NATIONALUM, VEDL, GRASIM) where the FMs say analyst-revisions are rising and momentum has stopped falling — plus a secondary financials/insurance lean (LICI, MUTHOOTFIN, banks). This is coherent and honestly reasoned: every FM independently flags that price-momentum and value z-scores are compressed near-flat this cross-section, so analyst-revision momentum (ARM, the ~1-6M tilt, IC~0.05, validated at IC 0.037-0.047 / t 2.4-5.0 in our own replay) is the only live differentiator, and each leans on it the right way (a small tilt synthesized with valuation/flow, never chased alone). The mandate identities are intact — large-cap core kept for benchmark integrity (ICICI), lower-beta hybrid ballast (SBI, the only book with β<1 at 0.86 and down-capture 0.86), flexi-cap breadth (ABSL ~62 names), momentum small-cap (Quant). My concern is not any single book; it is that the FIRM is making nearly the same single-factor, same-sector bet four times. Breadth at the firm level is thinner than the four mandates suggest: when ICICI, SBI and ABSL all top-weight ONGC/NMDC on the identical 'rising-revision commodity' thesis, those are not de-correlated desks — they are one desk expressed at three cap-sizes. The Fundamental-Law math says the firm's effective breadth (BR) is what should worry me, not its skill (IC is fine): correlated bets shrink √BR, so the firm IR is lower than the sum of the four book IRs implies. Verdict: keep the books as set — the reasoning is defensible and the tilts are individually sound — but recognize the firm is running a concentrated commodity/revision macro view, and the ONLY genuinely de-correlated desk is Quant Small Cap (distinct names HONASA/NAZARA/ARVIND/CHAMBLFERT/PIRAMALFIN, IR 0.92, the firm's true breadth contributor). I would not add more energy/materials at the firm level even if a fifth book wanted to.
⚠ Risk flags
  • SHARED-NAME CONCENTRATION: ONGC is a top target in ALL FOUR books; NMDC in three (ICICI/SBI/ABSL); COALINDIA, HINDALCO, NATIONALUM, LICI, MUTHOOTFIN, GRASIM each in two-plus. The firm's aggregate book is heavily loaded on a handful of PSU/commodity names — measure the consolidated firm-level weight in ONGC/NMDC/COALINDIA across the ₹1.6L cr+ of pilot AUM; it is almost certainly the largest single-name and single-sector exposure the firm has, and no individual book's sector cap sees it.
  • CROWDED SINGLE-FACTOR BET: all four FMs explicitly say the SAME thing — momentum/value are flat, so they are ALL tilting on analyst-revision (ARM) into the SAME cheap-commodity cluster. This is the textbook crowding setup: agreement of LEVEL (everyone piling into the same names on the same signal), which the CIO charter flags as fragility, not conviction. If the revision tide turns or the commodity cycle rolls, all four books de-rate together — the desks will co-draw-down, not diversify. ARM is a 1-6M signal with a fast decay; a crowded fast signal is the most fragile kind.
  • CROWDED x ILLIQUID FRAGILITY (small-cap): Quant Small Cap runs 102 tickets on 44 names at a momentum-led small-cap book with the worst drawdown in the firm (replay MaxDD -53%, bench -60%). Its top names (HONASA, NAZARA, ARVIND, CHAMBLFERT, PIRAMALFIN) are thin vs ADV; if revision-led flows reverse, the exit is disorderly. This is the firm's fire-sale corner — flag it as a fragility proxy (we have no leverage/positioning data, medium confidence only).
  • BETA SKEW IS ONE-SIDED RISK-ON: three of four books carry beta >1 (ICICI 1.09, ABSL 1.15, Quant ~0.92 but -53% DD) and all describe themselves 'risk-on'. Only SBI Hybrid is genuinely defensive (β0.86, down-capture 0.86). The firm has almost no dry powder / defensive ballast at the aggregate level — in a market drawdown the firm has only one book leaning against it.
  • MANDATE-DRIFT WATCH (mild): SBI Equity Hybrid's top targets include MEESHO 2.5% (TRIM) and LENSKART 2.4% — recently-listed, high-beta, thin-fundamental new-economy names sitting in a book that brands itself 'quality-tilted, lower-beta, defend on beta'. ABSL and Quant also carry LENSKART/HONASA/NAZARA. These names cut against the stated quality/ballast identity; not a breach, but watch that the 'value+revision' label isn't quietly housing momentum-IPO risk. Also note ICICI lists ICICIBANK twice in its top-targets line — confirm that is a display dedup, not a double-count, before sizing.
  • MACRO SINGLE-POINT-OF-FAILURE: the firm's dominant active bet is a leveraged view on the India commodity/energy/PSU-capex cycle (ONGC, COALINDIA, NMDC, NATIONALUM, HINDALCO, VEDL, power-capex). One macro turn — global commodity prices, a policy/levy shock to PSUs, or the revision cycle peaking — hits the energy/materials sleeve of THREE books at once. The firm is not diversified across macro regimes this round; it is long one regime.
Cross-scheme breadth
  • SHARED BETS (the consensus): ONGC = unanimous (4/4). NMDC = 3/4 (ICICI cyclical/NEW, SBI cyclical/NEW, ABSL cyclical/NEW). The cheap-commodity-revision cluster (COALINDIA, HINDALCO, NATIONALUM) and the financials/insurance lean (LICI, MUTHOOTFIN) recur across the three large/mid books. This is high conviction by COUNT but, per the CIO rule, agreement-of-level = crowding, not independent confirmation — discount it accordingly.
  • WHERE THE THESIS GENUINELY AGREES (and that's a tell): all four FMs independently diagnosed the SAME cross-section — momentum and value z-scores compressed flat, ARM the only live differentiator. Four independent desks reaching the identical read is either a real market state or a shared-signal artifact (they read the same quant baseline). Either way it means the firm has ONE view this round, not four.
  • WHERE THEY DIVERGE (the healthy part): Quant Small Cap is the genuinely de-correlated desk — its top names (HONASA, NAZARA, ARVIND, CHAMBLFERT, EQUITASBNK, PIRAMALFIN, KTKBANK) barely overlap the large/mid commodity cluster; it carries the firm's only distinct small-cap revision/momentum exposure and the firm's best stand-alone IR (0.92). It is doing the most for firm breadth. ABSL adds some divergence too (COFORGE/IT, GUJGASLTD gas) but its core still rhymes with ICICI/SBI on ONGC/NMDC/LICI/MUTHOOTFIN.
  • TRANSFER-COEFFICIENT SPREAD (skill vs implementation, per the Fundamental Law): the replay shows ICICI runs the tightest signal-to-book transfer (TC 0.82, the disciplined large-cap core) but the lowest realized IR (0.24, thin-edge mandate); ABSL and Quant deliberately run loose TC (0.37, 0.43 — heavy constraint/diversification leak) yet realize higher IR (0.50, 0.92) because their universes give more breadth. Read together: the firm's IR is being carried by Quant's breadth and ABSL's flexi mandate, NOT by the crowded large-cap commodity bet, where the edge is thinnest. That argues for protecting the small/flexi breadth, not adding to the large-cap commodity pile.
  • FIRM-BREADTH ACTION: to raise firm-level effective breadth (√BR) without touching the books, the loose thread is to ensure the next round's mandates don't ALL re-up the same commodity/revision cluster — the de-correlated desks (Quant small-cap, and ideally a quality/defensive or rates-sensitive lean) are what make the firm IR exceed the average book IR. Right now the firm is closer to one big book than four independent ones.
ICICI Pru Large Cap Fund (G)37 names · 95.0% deployed · 68 trades · turnover 99.3%
Benchmark-aware large-cap core with a measured tilt to multi-signal value+momentum+rising-revision names (energy/materials/power-capex), deliberately taming the quant baseline's extended-momentum and capacity-thin chases.
This is a NIFTY-100 Large Cap mandate where the realized edge is thin (IR ~0.24) and beta already runs hot at 1.09, so the job is to harvest small, repeatable tilts on top of a defensible large-cap core rather than manufacture alpha by chasing the most extended momentum. I lean into names where analyst revisions, relative momentum and cheapness AGREE — large, liquid energy and materials (ONGC, NMDC, NATIONALUM, COALINDIA, HINDALCO, VEDL) plus selective industrials/power-capex and exchanges (CGPOWER, BSE, MCX) — and I overweight them modestly. I keep the core banks, IT, FMCG, pharma and Reliance for benchmark integrity even where their signals are soft, because abandoning them is uncompensated tracking risk in a thin-edge book. I trim or exit falling-revision value traps (Tata Motors entities, HDFCAMC) and refuse the baseline's most capacity-questionable, single-signal momentum spikes (MTARTECH, HFCL, ATHERENERG, MEESHO, GROWW), since their extreme momentum z-scores are not confirmed by valuation or breadth and would amplify an already high-beta book. Net: a slightly lower-beta, more multi-force-confirmed expression of the desk view, staying within the 35-55 name band and well under sector caps.

Pre-registered bets — thesis & falsifier (anti-hindsight)
ONGC cyclical 5.0%
ONGC pairs strong, rising analyst revisions with top-decile cheapness and a stable large-cap energy profile; the cheapness gives downside support while revisions provide the 1-6mo tilt.
✗ wrong if: Crude and gas realizations roll over and analyst revisions turn falling, or the stock de-rates further despite cheapness over the next two quarters.
VEDL structural 5.0%
Diversified metals exposure with confirming momentum and rising revisions; a structural commodity-cycle and deleveraging story I already own at size.
✗ wrong if: Metals prices fall and revisions roll over, or balance-sheet/dividend concerns re-emerge, taking momentum negative.
ICICIBANK structural 4.4%
n/a
✗ wrong if: n/a

NMDC cyclical 3.2%
NMDC shows strong rising analyst revisions and above-peer cheapness with neutral-to-positive momentum — a confirmed materials tilt, not a single-signal chase.
✗ wrong if: Iron ore prices and revisions both weaken, or volumes disappoint and momentum turns clearly negative.
NTPC structural 3.2%
NTPC is a cheap, large-cap regulated-return utility with a capacity-addition growth angle and mildly rising revisions — defensive carry in the book.
✗ wrong if: Regulatory returns are cut, capex disappoints, or revisions turn falling alongside weak momentum.

SBI Equity Hybrid Fund (G)34 names · 72.5% deployed · 53 trades · turnover 48.3%
Quality-tilted risk-on within the hybrid mandate: lean into the cheap energy/materials value-plus-rising-revisions cluster, keep the bank/compounder core, prune the falling-revision tail.
As an aggressive-hybrid book I keep a lower-beta, large/mid quality identity but tilt the equity sleeve toward the one fresh, repeatable edge visible in the sheet this rebalance: a cluster of cheap commodity/energy and select large-caps where analyst revisions are clearly RISING and momentum is no longer falling (ONGC, NMDC, COALINDIA, HINDALCO, NATIONALUM, GRASIM, LICI, GLAND, ZYDUSLIFE). I hold the franchise banks and structural compounders as ballast but de-size the ones whose revisions have gone flat-to-down (HDFCBANK, KOTAKBANK, ITC), and I exit the falling-revision, weak-momentum tail (LT, INDIGO, JSWENERGY, ADANIENSOL, SWIGGY, DIVISLAB, MAXHEALTH, ADANIPOWER) plus the sub-0.5% legacy stubs that cannot move the book. ARM is treated as a small 1-6 month tilt synthesized with valuation, momentum and flows — never chased alone; I deliberately avoid the momentum-blowup micro names (STLTECH, MTARTECH) as off-mandate. The result is a 40-odd name book inside the count band, sector concentration kept well under the cap, that should defend on beta while harvesting the value+revision tilt.

Pre-registered bets — thesis & falsifier (anti-hindsight)
ONGC cyclical 3.3%
A cheap, liquid large-cap where analyst revisions are strongly rising and price momentum is turning up; classic value-plus-revision re-rating candidate that fits a lower-beta hybrid.

Cash-generative, cheap large-cap with rising revisions and steady momentum; defensive cyclical with a yield cushion.

Aditya Birla SL Flexi Cap Fund (G)62 names · 95.0% deployed · 79 trades · turnover 49.2%
Risk-aware flexi-cap: keep the high-conviction quality/cyclical core, tilt toward names with strong/rising analyst revisions, and prune the clearly-falling-revision laggards while adding liquid large/mid ballast to temper a hot beta.
In this cross-section, price-momentum and value z-scores are nearly flat across the universe, so the one signal carrying real differentiation is analyst-revision momentum (a small ~1-6 month tilt, IC ~0.05, never a guarantee). I therefore tilt the book toward names where revisions are strong or rising and rotate capital away from holdings where revisions have clearly turned down, without chasing any single name. Because this is a ~₹26,000 cr book benchmarked to NIFTY 500 with a measured beta of 1.15, I deliberately keep liquid large- and mid-cap financials, IT and consumer staples as ballast and trim or exit the most beta-additive small-cap laggards, aiming to lift information ratio by improving the quality of bets rather than adding more risk. I hold near the top of the 40-70 name band for breadth (de-correlated bets raise effective breadth), respect the 9% per-name and 35% per-sector spirit with weights well inside those caps, and treat the quant baseline as a reference - I keep its strong-revision ideas but override its small-cap, capacity-blind concentration.

Quant Small Cap Fund - (G)35 names · 95.0% deployed · 101 trades · turnover 123.7%
Risk-on within mandate: momentum-led small-cap, tilt hard into rising analyst-revisions, cut the laggard tail.
This is a momentum-led small-cap book run at large AUM, so the edge has to come from where small-cap signals are still discriminating. Right now price-momentum is compressed almost flat across the small-cap universe (cross-sectional momentum z-scores cluster near zero), so the cleanest live differentiator is analyst-revision breadth: a tight cluster of names is seeing strong, rising earnings revisions while a long tail of my legacy holdings is seeing falling revisions with no momentum offset. I therefore rotate the book toward the strong-revision cluster the quant baseline also favors (financiers, fertilizer/gas, pharma/CDMO, select consumer and capital-goods names), keep the held winners that still carry rising revisions, and cut or sharply trim the deep-laggard tail (deteriorating revisions plus weak momentum) that only adds beta and turnover drag. I deliberately do not chase the one or two extreme momentum outliers (thin diversified names) because they are illiquid oddballs unsupported by revisions. Net effect: a slightly more concentrated ~60-name book, still well within the holding band and sector caps, with conviction placed where rising revisions and acceptable relative momentum overlap. Revisions are treated as a small 1-6 month tilt (IC~0.05), not a guarantee, so no single name is oversized.
