Rare large-cap Quadrant-1 that is STILL a value name: ARM maxed, earnings accelerating, funds already net buyers but valuation un-stretched. The small ARM tilt is reinforced not fighting the tape; clean upstream exposure for a value book.
take NMDC core from Metals & Materials
Exactly my setup: top-ARM large-cap miner, steady earnings, QUIET fund accumulation - Street upgrading but crowd has NOT yet piled in (early Q1). Dividend-yield value miner, low valuation, sized before flow confirms.
take COALINDIA starter from Energy & Power
Dividend-yield value anchor with strong ARM upgrade momentum. PAT is contracting so I size it as a starter not a core - ARM tilt vs weak trailing earnings is a real conflict, kept small per the desk caveat.
take HINDZINC starter from Metals & Materials
Cleanest Q1 large-cap metal where ARM, flow and earnings all agree on a value/yield miner; both independent signals confirm. Adds metals breadth without a momentum-chase premium.
take ZYDUSLIFE starter from Pharma & Healthcare
Textbook contra entry routed to me: strongest revision among large generics but funds NOT yet buying (Q2 analysts-ahead). A cheap large-cap generic to accumulate ON confirmation - starter now, add as MF flow turns.
pass WIPRO
Tagged Value & Contra but it is a value TRAP, not a value buy: funds dumping at scale, earnings flat, ARM stale against the exodus. The analyst himself calls it a source-of-funds. No intact underlying case to be contrarian about - I pass; would only revisit if flow stabilises and ARM re-accelerates.
pass VEDL
ARM 70 but MFs dumped -13,051cr (order-of-magnitude desk-largest distribution) on de-merger/overhang risk. Contrarian only AFTER flow reverses; a -13k cr exodus is a falling knife, not yet a fade-the-crowd opportunity. Escalated, not taken.
pass HINDPETRO
Q3 trap: huge reported PAT + heavy fund buying but ARM at floor (2) - Street fading the OMC marketing-margin windfall. I side with the revisions; the earnings are a cyclical spike, not durable value.
pass BIOCON
Cleanest negative on the board - analysts cutting, funds selling, earnings collapsing simultaneously. No contra case until the triple turns; avoiding a falling fundamental is not contrarianism.
Escalated to CIO
conflict VEDL -13,051cr fund exodus vs ARM 70 — Largest single-name distribution on any desk against a healthy ARM - is this a de-merger/overhang the Street has not marked down, or a contra entry once flow reverses? Need CIO read on the de-merger structure before any value desk acts.
large-size LICI force divergence (Q2, highest ARM in coverage, funds net sellers) — Large, contentious index-heavy contra: highest analyst revision in coverage but MFs selling and FII flat. Given ARM IC is thin I want flow confirmation before a meaningful weight - confirm sizing band given its benchmark heft.
Investor experience
Book is built for a SITTABLE path, not a hero year. Energy/metals value tilt is cyclical, so I cap any single sector at benchmark+ a few points and keep tracking error in the exemplar 3.3-4.7% band to avoid a deep relative drawdown. Dividend-yield names (NMDC, COALINDIA, HINDZINC) carry the carry and cushion downside while the contra theses mature. I deliberately decline the four value-traps that look cheap but are deteriorating - the fastest way to break investor trust in a value fund is to confuse a falling knife with a bargain. Consistency target ~60% rolling-period hit-rate (exemplar Invesco/Canara band); IC is thin so edge is patience and breadth, and I will accept lagging the momentum tape in a risk-on month as the price of a defensible, mandate-true contra book.
📈 Focused Strategy · Fund Manager
balanced
28 peer funds · category median IR 0.4 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Mahindra Manulife Focused Fund (G): IR 1.33, IC -0.027 (t -1.0), TE 3.8% — skilled
A concentrated ~25-28 name book (SEBI Focused cap = 30) run against NIFTY 500, quality-tilted, tracking error targeted at the lower exemplar band (~4-5%, vs Mahindra 3.8 / ITI 5.2). My edge is NOT per-name forecasting precision - the skilled Focused exemplars show near-zero revealed IC (Mahindra -0.027, ITI +0.006, Quant +0.043) yet IR 0.75-1.33 - so the alpha comes from CONCENTRATION in durable Quadrant-1 compounders held at conviction weight (transfer-coefficient), not from churning a wide cross-section. With structurally low breadth I cannot diversify away a single blow-up, so every slot must be an earnings-backed, both-forces-positive name; I overweight a handful of large/mid quality compounders, deliberately avoid speculative small-cap ARM torque, and use the few flow-vs-analyst divergences only as escalations, not core bets.
Book tilt
Concentrated quality-compounder core: ICICIBANK / TITAN / TVSMOTOR / APOLLOHOSP / COFORGE / BHARTIARTL held at conviction weight - transfer/conviction over per-name IC · Quadrant-1, both-forces-confirmed only in core slots; analyst-ahead (Q2) names parked as watch, never sized in a low-breadth book · Tracking error targeted ~4-5% (lower exemplar band: Mahindra 3.8 / ITI 5.2), not the 9.3 Quant style · Deliberately avoid speculative small-cap ARM torque (PREMIERENE/MRPL/BSE/HONASA) - low breadth cannot absorb a single blow-up · Active vs NIFTY 500: overweight private financials + discretionary/healthcare quality, underweight slow-growth large-cap IT (WIPRO) and flow-only names (RELIANCE/MARUTI)
Pitches taken / passed
take ICICIBANK overweight from Banking & Financials
Heaviest active fund accumulation in the financials desk plus supportive ARM, clean Quadrant 1, 3-6M clock. Lowest-volatility way to take my single largest slot - a core private bank is the right anchor for a concentrated NIFTY-500 book and lets me carry index-sized financials weight in ONE high-conviction name rather than many.
take TITAN core from Consumer
Cleanest large-cap discretionary compounder, Quadrant 1 with no signal conflict, 3-6M clock, earnings/flow/revisions all aligned. Exactly the durable-compounder profile a focused book monetises through transfer/conviction rather than IC.
take TVSMOTOR core from Auto & Mobility
The only Quadrant-1 name in Auto where analyst revisions AND funds both point up (rest of the sector is being trimmed); premiumisation/EV showing in earnings, high ARM, 3-6M. A both-forces-confirmed quality name - my preferred single Auto expression over flow-only Maruti (#24) or analyst-only Bharat Forge (#22).
take APOLLOHOSP core from Pharma & Healthcare
Best-aligned large-cap on the pharma desk and the cleanest structural healthcare-delivery compounder; every signal positive, no PAT or flow blemish, 3-6M. A differentiated, low-overlap quality slot for a NIFTY-500-relative book.
take COFORGE core from IT & Technology
Cleanest triple-confirm in IT - top ARM, heavy fund accumulation, explosive earnings, Quadrant 1. My single IT expression: I take the upgraded quality mid-cap rather than spreading thin across HEXT/MPHASIS/TECHM, and explicitly source funds from WIPRO (analyst signal stale vs MF exodus).
take BHARTIARTL core from Telecom & Media
Sector anchor with overwhelming smart-money flow (+Rs8.5k cr/3m) and rising revisions; strongest FII build in coverage, 3-6M. A core large-cap hold - but I size to a full-not-overweight slot until the soft headline PAT delta is checked, since the optical earnings is the one caveat.
pass EICHERMOT
Routed to Focused and a genuine quality franchise, but it is Quadrant 2 (analysts ahead, funds NOT confirming - Rs339cr outflow) on a 6-12M clock. In a 30-slot book I will not spend a precious slot on an unconfirmed flow setup when I already own confirmed Quadrant-1 quality in TVSMOTOR; track for re-entry once MF flow turns.
pass RELIANCE
Routed to Focused and the desk's largest fund accumulation, but the analyst's own call is HOLD with only NEUTRAL revisions - flow/franchise, not an ARM tilt. I would only own it as a benchmark-risk-control weight, not a conviction slot; in a concentrated book I prefer to spend the slot on a both-forces-positive name and keep Reliance underweight vs index.
pass HEXT
Clean Quadrant-1 mid-cap IT, but it is a near-duplicate of my COFORGE thesis. Concentration discipline: I take ONE best-in-class IT expression rather than stacking correlated mid-cap IT bets that add no independent breadth to a low-breadth book.
pass PREMIERENE
Strong Quadrant-1 triple-confirm, but it is a small/mid solar-manufacturing cyclical on a short ARM clock - exactly the speculative torque a low-breadth Focused book cannot diversify against a single blow-up. Better suited to a small-cap or quant sleeve, not my concentrated quality slots.
Escalated to CIO
conflict WIPRO as a source of funds for COFORGE within IT — WIPRO (#5) shows a stale-decent ARM against a large MF exodus and flat earnings. I want to fund my COFORGE add by trimming/exiting WIPRO to benchmark-underweight. Confirm the desk view that the ARM is genuinely stale (not early) so I am not selling into an analyst signal that is about to be confirmed.
mandate BHARTIARTL optical PAT before sizing to full weight — The analyst flags the soft headline PAT as 'optical' against a +Rs8.5k cr/3m fund bid. Before I lift Bharti from a full slot toward overweight in a concentrated book, I need the normalised/ex-exceptional PAT delta confirmed - sizing a top-5 slot on an unverified earnings caveat is a quality-control question for the book.
large-size Single-name bank concentration - ICICIBANK as my largest slot — To keep tracking error in the ~4-5% band I plan to express my entire core financials overweight through ICICIBANK rather than splitting into MUTHOOTFIN/POLICYBZR/BSE. Confirm single-name bank concentration at overweight is within the desk's stock and sector risk limits for a Focused mandate.
Investor experience
Consistency is the deliverable, not max CAGR: the skilled Focused exemplars hit 54-67% rolling-period hit-rate at +6-8%/yr excess, which is what a concentrated book earns by HOLDING quality, not trading it. By restricting core slots to both-forces-positive large/mid compounders and refusing small-cap ARM torque, I cap the single-name drawdown risk that a 25-28 stock book cannot diversify away. The two earnings-quality caveats I am escalating (Bharti optical PAT, Wipro stale ARM) are deliberately handled BEFORE sizing so a bad print does not become a deep, slow-recovery hole in a concentrated portfolio. Net intended experience: smoother path, shallower drawdowns than the Quant-style high-TE focused funds, with category-relative outperformance that compounds rather than spikes.
📈 ELSS (Tax Saver) Strategy · Fund Manager
balanced
57 peer funds · category median IR 0.44 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Mirae Asset ELSS Tax Saver Fund - Regular (G): IR 1.29, IC 0.009 (t 0.7), TE 2.9% — skilled
Run a Mirae/DSP-template ELSS book vs NIFTY 500: tracking error targeted ~3.5-4.5%, edge from BREADTH x hit-rate, not concentration. The exemplar evidence is decisive — Mirae earns IR 1.29 on TE 2.9% with ic_mean ~0 and a 64% hit rate; the skill is construction/transfer, not big per-name IC. So I tilt toward many small, triple-confirmed (Quadrant-1) quality compounders with clean PAT and broad sector spread for de-correlated breadth, sized as starters/cores, and I refuse hero bets. The 3-year statutory lock-in means no forced redemptions, so I can hold the 1-6M ARM clock to maturity. Beating the 0.44 category-median IR comes from a >60% hit rate across 18-25 well-diversified Q1 names, not from a few high-torque small-caps. Skew large/mid quality; treat ARM as a small confirming tilt only where flow AND earnings agree.
Book tilt
Quality large/mid Quadrant-1 compounders; ARM used only as a confirming tilt where flow+earnings agree · Tracking error held ~3.5-4.5% vs NIFTY 500 — breadth x hit-rate, not concentration (Mirae/DSP template) · De-correlated sector spread (IT, financials, consumer, healthcare, metals, auto) to raise effective breadth · Underweight single-force/Q4 names (WIPRO, LT) and force-divergence risks (VEDL); avoid speculative small/micro-caps · Large-cap liquidity bias — exploit the 3-yr ELSS lock-in to hold the 1-6M ARM clock without redemption pressure
Pitches taken / passed
take COFORGE core from IT & Technology
Cleanest triple-confirm on the IT desk (top ARM + heavy fund accumulation + explosive PAT, Quadrant 1). Mid-cap quality compounder where all three independent forces agree — exactly the breadth-additive Q1 name my hit-rate book wants. ARM tilt is reinforced by flow+earnings, not fighting them.
take ICICIBANK core from Banking & Financials
Core large-cap private bank, Quadrant 1 with the heaviest active fund accumulation in financials and supportive ARM. Low idiosyncratic risk, anchors the book near benchmark, keeps TE controlled while still being a confirmed both-forces-positive add. NIFTY 500 heavyweight — owning it as an active overweight is low-tracking-error skill expression.
take TITAN core from Consumer
Cleanest large-cap discretionary compounder, Q1 with no signal conflict (revisions + flow + earnings aligned). Quality, liquid, defensible — a steady contributor that supports hit rate without adding path roughness.
take APOLLOHOSP core from Pharma & Healthcare
Best-aligned large-cap on the pharma desk, every signal positive with no PAT/flow blemish. Structural healthcare-delivery theme, liquid large-cap — adds de-correlated breadth (healthcare) at low idiosyncratic risk.
take HINDZINC starter from Metals & Materials
Cleanest Q1 large-cap in metals — ARM, fund buying and earnings all aligned. Adds a de-correlated cyclical sleeve to the book (metals is uncorrelated with IT/financials/consumer), improving effective breadth per the Fundamental Law. Sized as a starter to cap commodity beta.
take TVSMOTOR starter from Auto & Mobility
The only Quadrant-1 name in Auto where ARM and fund flow both point up, with premiumisation/EV showing in earnings. Large/mid quality, liquid — a clean confirmed add that diversifies into autos while the rest of the sector shows flow outflows. Starter size to keep auto beta modest.
pass HAL
Genuinely attractive Q1 defence megacap (flow + revisions agree) and a strong breadth candidate — parked as the next add, not declined on merit. Held back this round only to keep the book at ~18-25 names and TE within target; would size it as a starter on the next rebalance over the smaller/cyclical industrials names.
pass BSE
Smaller-cap, higher-conviction growth name with only modest fund buying. ~88% PAT is a derivatives-volume-cycle print — high single-name volatility. Adds path roughness and TE for a 1-3M clock; my hit-rate template prefers the liquid Q1 banks (ICICIBANK already taken).
pass PREMIERENE
Solar-manufacturing up-cycle name straddling mid/small-cap — high torque, high cyclicality, policy-sensitive. Even as a Q1 triple-confirm it carries single-name drawdown risk an ELSS hit-rate book should avoid; I'd rather express industrials via HAL's liquidity.
pass HONASA
Low conviction, small/micro-cap, explicitly flagged as a speculative ARM print on a momentum sleeve. Liquidity and single-name drawdown risk are wrong for a tax-saver investor-experience mandate.
Escalated to CIO
conflict VEDL — ARM 70 vs -13,051 cr fund distribution (largest on the metals desk) — This is the loudest force-divergence on the board and a NIFTY 500 constituent with de-merger/overhang risk. Confirm the firm-wide stance: I am defaulting to NOT holding (underweight) until flow reverses, but want CIO sign-off given its index heft and the order-of-magnitude flow against a decent ARM.
mandate LT — Quadrant-4 sector bellwether and major NIFTY 500 / infra-index weight — LT anchors infra and large-cap indices, so an active underweight materially affects my tracking error. Analysts not revising up and funds net sellers (Q4) argue underweight, but its benchmark heft makes this a TE-mandate question — confirm how far below benchmark weight I may run it without breaching the ~4.5% TE target.
Investor experience
Mandate prizes CONSISTENT category-relative outperformance and a sittable path, and the lock-in removes redemption risk — so I optimize for hit rate and shallow drawdown, not peak CAGR. Target hit rate >60% (Mirae 64%, DSP 61%) by holding 18-25 diversified Q1 names so no single position can drive a deep relative drawdown; that is why I size COFORGE/ICICIBANK/TITAN/APOLLO as cores and the cyclicals/higher-beta names (HINDZINC, TVSMOTOR) as starters. I deliberately avoid the Quant-ELSS profile (TE 8.9%, hit 56%) — higher absolute return but a rough path that hurts the tax-saver investor's lived experience. Net: a balanced, broadly-diversified book that should track close to NIFTY 500 with a steady positive excess, modest tracking error, and no hero-position tail risk.
📈 Dividend Yield Strategy · Fund Manager
balanced
10 peer funds · category median IR 0.45 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar HDFC Dividend Yield Fund (G): IR 1.25, IC 0.007 (t 0.3), TE 4.0% — skilled
Low tracking-error tilt (target TE ~4-5%, in line with category exemplars at 4.0-5.7%) toward cash-generative, high-dividend-yield value: overweight upstream energy and PSU resource/yield names, defensive FMCG ballast, underweight the IT/CDMO/solar/fintech growth-momentum complex that dominates the pitch board. The Fundamental Law drives this: every Dividend Yield exemplar posts a strong IR (0.78-1.25, median category 0.45) on a revealed per-name IC of ~0 or negative (HDFC +0.007 t=0.3, Tata -0.017, LIC -0.004) — so this category's edge is a persistent STYLE TILT + high hit rate (57-65%), NOT revision-momentum stock-picking. I therefore use ARM (IC ~0.03-0.045, 1-6M clock) only as a tie-breaker among names I'd own for yield/cash-flow anyway, never to chase a low-yield compounder.
Book tilt
Overweight high-dividend-yield upstream energy & PSU resources (ONGC, NMDC) · Defensive FMCG ballast for drawdown control (TATACONSUM) · Underweight IT/CDMO/solar/fintech growth-momentum complex (off-mandate) · Avoid yield traps where ARM upgrades fight contracting PAT/exiting flow (COALINDIA watch, VEDL out) · Low tracking error ~4-5%, value/cash-flow style coherence over per-name ARM bets
Pitches taken / passed
take ONGC core from Energy & Power
Rare yield name with ARM tailwind not fighting it: large-cap upstream, among the highest index dividend yields, Quadrant-1 (ARM maxed + funds net buyers + earnings accelerating). The small ARM tilt REINFORCES a name I'd own for cash flow anyway, so it earns a core weight; best risk-reward for a yield book.
take NMDC core from Metals & Materials
Explicitly Dividend-Yield-tagged, Quadrant-1 high-yield PSU miner: top ARM, steady earnings, quiet (early) fund accumulation so I can build before the crowd chases. Clean value/yield fit; low style risk.
take TATACONSUM starter from Consumer
Dividend-Yield-tagged FMCG staple, Quadrant-1 with the best earnings growth among FMCG large-caps and analysts+funds onside. Defensive ballast that improves the book's drawdown/consistency profile without the distribution overhang on Nestle/Asian Paints. Lower yield than the PSUs, so sized as a starter for stability, not yield.
pass COALINDIA
Dividend-Yield-tagged and a classic high-yield PSU, but the analyst flags EARNINGS CONTRACTING while ARM upgrades. For a yield mandate the dividend's cash-flow durability IS the thesis; a small ARM tilt (IC ~0.03) cannot override deteriorating PAT in a name held for its payout. Watch for PAT confirmation, do not add.
pass RELIANCE
Anchor franchise with strong flow, but low dividend yield and ARM only neutral — it does not express the yield mandate and would dilute style coherence. A flow/franchise hold for other desks, not a yield-book core.
pass COFORGE
Clean Quadrant-1 mid-cap quality compounder, but a low-yield growth/momentum name — categorically off a Dividend Yield mandate regardless of triple-confirm. Excellent idea for a growth desk; owning it would break my style tilt and inflate tracking error.
pass TRENT
Strong flow-confirmed retail growth, but a high-multiple low-yield discretionary compounder — off-mandate for a dividend-yield book. Belongs on a Flexi/Consumption desk, not here.
Escalated to CIO
conflict VEDL — high-yield name with a -13,051 cr mutual-fund exodus against a healthy ARM 70 — VEDL screens into dividend-yield universes but shows the desk's loudest flow-vs-revision divergence; flow this large vs a decent ARM signals de-merger/overhang risk the Street hasn't marked. Confirm I should keep it OUT of the yield book until flow reverses, and whether the firm wants a coordinated stance given its index/yield heft.
Investor experience
The category edge is consistency, not flashes — exemplars hit 57-65% of periods with TE only 4.0-5.7%, IR up to 1.25 on ~zero stock-selection IC. So I optimize the PATH: pair high-yield cyclicals (ONGC, NMDC) with a defensive FMCG payer (TATACONSUM) to dampen the energy/metals drawdown, keep ARM as a tie-breaker not a driver, and refuse yield traps (COALINDIA contracting earnings, VEDL flow exodus) that cause the deep, slow-recovery drawdowns yield investors won't sit through. Goal: steady category-relative outperformance with a smooth ride, not a high-variance ARM chase.
📈 Banking & Financials Thematic · Fund Manager
balanced
26 peer funds · category median IR 0.37 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Bank of India Manufacturing & Infrastr Fund (G): IR 0.6, IC -0.002 (t -0.1), TE 9.2% — skilled
Category-relative outperformance on this desk is empirically a sub-sector and avoid-blowups game, not a name-picking game: all three exemplar funds post positive IR (0.44-0.60) on essentially zero/negative revealed selection IC (-0.002 to -0.028), so by IR = IC x sqrt(BR) x TC their edge is the financials tilt vs a broad NIFTY 500 benchmark, not stock alpha. I therefore run a quality private-bank core (low tracking error, the reliable compounder) and express conviction through a measured overweight to confirmed Quadrant-1 NBFC/fintech growth where ARM AND fund flow agree, while declining analyst-ahead (Quadrant-2) and force-divergent names that would add tracking error without confirmed edge. Target tracking error well inside the 7.7-9.3 exemplar band (~5-6%), book tilted to private banks + selective high-quality NBFCs, deliberately underweight the speculative micro-cap and the unconfirmed insurer.
Book tilt
Overweight quality private banks (ICICIBANK core) - the low-tracking-error consistency anchor · Selective high-quality NBFC growth where ARM AND earnings confirm (MUTHOOTFIN core, gold-loan cycle) · Small capital-markets-infra torque sleeve (BSE starter) on the volume/derivatives cycle · Underweight unconfirmed/divergent names: no LICI (Q2 no-flow), no POLICYBZR (thin-IC high-beta) · Tracking error targeted ~5-6%, inside the 7.7-9.3 exemplar band - tilt expressed through sub-sector, not single-name bets
Pitches taken / passed
take ICICIBANK core from Banking & Financials
Quadrant-1 large-cap private bank: heaviest active fund accumulation in the sector plus supportive ARM, both independent forces aligned. This is my low-tracking-error anchor - the name that delivers category-relative consistency without leaning on a per-name ARM guarantee. Add into, do not chase.
take MUTHOOTFIN core from Banking & Financials
Top-decile ARM with near-doubling of profit - revisions backed by realized earnings, not hope. Gold-loan cycle upgrade is the cleanest quality-NBFC expression on the desk; held core not overweight to respect the breadth term - a single gold-price-sensitive bet on a 1-3M clock should not concentrate tracking error.
take BSE starter from Banking & Financials
Capital-markets-infra with high ARM and ~88% PAT growth on the derivatives/volume cycle; fund buying modest-but-positive (Quadrant-1). Real earnings behind the revision, but smaller-cap and cycle-sensitive - a starter, sized small as a higher-torque sleeve, not a core hold.
pass POLICYBZR
Higher-beta fintech with a profit inflection, but the pitch itself frames it as a risk-on expression. ARM IC is ~0.03-0.04 - too thin to justify the added tracking error from a still-young, high-multiple insurtech when I already hold the BSE torque sleeve. Pass for consistency; revisit if flow scales.
pass LICI
Quadrant-2 force divergence: highest ARM in coverage but mutual funds are net sellers and FII flat. With ARM's modest IC, an analyst-ahead signal without flow confirmation is exactly the low-conviction tilt that adds tracking error for no proven edge. Decline now; the analyst already routed it to CIO, so I defer rather than size.
Escalated to CIO
conflict LICI sizing (Quadrant-2, large contentious insurer) — Analyst routed LICI to CIO on a force divergence - highest ARM in coverage but funds net sellers, FII flat. I decline to size it on the analyst-led signal alone given ARM's ~0.03-0.04 IC. Confirm the CIO wants flow confirmation before any desk takes it, or whether a small index-relative weight is warranted given its NIFTY 500 heft.
Investor experience
The investor experience is built on consistency, not heroics: a private-bank core (ICICIBANK) keeps drawdown and tracking error contained while two confirmed-growth sleeves (MUTHOOTFIN core, BSE starter) provide measured upside on a 1-3M ARM clock. I deliberately declined the speculative/divergent names (LICI Q2, POLICYBZR thin-IC) because the desk's own exemplars prove category-relative IR comes from disciplined sub-sector tilt and avoiding blow-ups (IC ~0, IR 0.44-0.60), not from chasing every high-ARM print. Hit-rate target ~57-60% (exemplar band) with shallower relative drawdowns; ARM is treated as a small 1-6M tilt that reinforces flow-confirmed names, never a per-name guarantee.
📈 Pharma & Healthcare Thematic · Fund Manager
balanced
17 peer funds · category median IR 0.48 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar DSP Healthcare Fund (G): IR 0.59, IC 0.02 (t 0.8), TE 14.5% — ahead but not yet significant
Category-relative book benchmarked to NIFTY 500 (pharma exemplar median IR 0.48, TE 10-15%/yr). The Healthcare desk is the cleanest in the market right now — equal-weight ARM 56.9 (2nd-highest sector) with 28 of 68 names in Quadrant 1 (both analyst-revision and fund-flow positive), the widest clean-confirm breadth of any desk. That breadth is exactly what the Fundamental Law rewards: with per-name ARM IC only ~0.03-0.045, edge has to come from sqrt(breadth), not hero bets. So I run a diversified quality-and-delivery overweight (CDMO + hospitals + large-cap pharma) of ~9-11 names, each starter-to-core, none over-concentrated. I tilt AWAY from the loss-making/triple-negative tail (BIOCON, COHANCE, PPLPHARMA) and AWAY from the heavy-distribution Q2 generics where flow is fighting the tape (LUPIN flow -1975, GLAND -579). Mandate constraints respected: cap band stays diversified across large (APOLLOHOSP, SUNPHARMA), mid (ANTHEM, LAURUSLABS) and a small quality sleeve (THYROCARE), and aggregate active tracking error kept inside the ~12% category norm by sizing single-name Q2 'analysts-ahead' bets as starters until flow confirms.
Book tilt
Overweight CDMO/CRAMS quality (ANTHEM core, LAURUSLABS starter) — Q1/Q2 with the strongest PAT inflections (+31% to +148%) on the desk · Overweight hospitals/delivery (APOLLOHOSP) to diversify away from US-FDA/generics regulatory beta · Benchmark-anchored large-cap core (SUNPHARMA, APOLLOHOSP) to keep tracking error inside the ~12% category norm · Underweight heavy-distribution Q2 generics (LUPIN -1975, GLAND -579) where flow fights the ARM · Zero exposure to the triple-negative tail (BIOCON, COHANCE, PPLPHARMA) — loss-making, ARM<16, funds selling
Pitches taken / passed
take ANTHEM core from Healthcare analyst
Cleanest triple-confirm on my desk and the rare Q1 mid-cap CDMO: ARM 79, fund flow +899 cr (3rd-heaviest accumulation in coverage), PAT +31.3% YoY, no offsetting red flag. All three independent forces agree, so the small ARM tilt is reinforced not fighting flow. Core CDMO position.
take APOLLOHOSP core from Healthcare analyst
Best-aligned large-cap on the desk: ARM 57, flow +618 cr, PAT +33% YoY, Q1, FII +42.6. Hospital/delivery is a structural non-FDA-risk theme that diversifies my book away from pure-generics regulatory beta. Anchor large-cap holding sized to core.
take LAURUSLABS starter from Healthcare analyst
Best growth+momentum combo in coverage: ARM 80, PAT +147.9% YoY (2nd-best on desk), FII +25.8. Q2 'analysts ahead' — MF flow is only -65 cr (essentially flat, NOT distribution), so I take it as a starter and add when MF flow turns positive to confirm the earnings inflection.
take SUNPHARMA core from Healthcare analyst desk (top-by-mcap)
Largest desk holding by mcap and the heaviest fund accumulation in coverage (+3069 cr), Q1, FII +15.9. ARM only 50 so I do not lean on it for the revision tilt, but as the sector anchor with overwhelming smart-money flow it is a benchmark-aware core hold that caps tracking error vs a pharma index.
take THYROCARE starter from Healthcare analyst desk (growth-leaders)
Clean small-cap Q1: ARM 81, flow +27.5 cr, PAT +79.1% YoY. Diagnostics/delivery exposure with all three forces aligned; sized as a starter in a small-cap quality sleeve to add an independent bet (breadth) without liquidity risk at scale.
pass BIOCON
Agree with the AVOID — cleanest triple-negative on the desk: ARM 10, flow -994 cr (2nd-heaviest distribution), PAT -74.2% YoY, Q4. Not recommending until the triple turns.
pass ZYDUSLIFE
Q2 divergence: highest ARM in coverage (91) but funds net sellers (-160 cr), FII flat (+6.9), weak PAT +9.7%. Given ARM's ~0.03 IC I want flow confirmation before sizing a large-cap generic; on watch, not in the book.
pass LUPIN
Heaviest desk distribution (flow -1975 cr) while Q2, despite ARM 63 / PAT +62%. Flow that large against the revision is exactly what I avoid leading with — the tape is fighting the estimate.
pass GLAND
Top ARM in coverage (96) and PAT +46.7%, but flow -579 cr and FII only +7.3 — Q2 with funds exiting. High-ARM-but-distributed is a value-trap risk until flow stabilizes.
Escalated to CIO
conflict ZYDUSLIFE large-cap generic — analyst-ahead vs flow divergence — Analyst routed it up: highest ARM in coverage (91) but MF flow -160 cr and FII flat (+6.9), Q2. Do we want a benchmark-aware starter on revision strength, or hold for flow confirmation? I lean hold; seeking CIO read on whether revision alone justifies a position given ARM's ~0.03 IC on a large, contentious generics name.
Investor experience
Consistency comes from breadth, not concentration: 9-11 names sized starter-to-core means no single ARM miss (a ~0.03-0.045 IC, 1-6 month signal — never a per-name guarantee) sinks the book. I deliberately favour Q1 names where fund flow already corroborates the revision, which dampens drawdown vs chasing high-ARM-but-distributed names (LUPIN/GLAND/ZYDUS) that can de-rate even as estimates rise. Hospital exposure (APOLLOHOSP) lowers correlation to the generics regulatory cycle, smoothing the path. Honest caveat for the investor: the pharma exemplars (DSP IR 0.59 / IC 0.02 t=0.8, Mirae IR 0.49 / IC 0.012 t=0.5, ITI IR 0.48 / IC -0.029 t=-1.1) all show revealed IC near or below zero with t<1 — sector funds here add value mostly via thematic beta and timing, not yet statistically-proven stock selection. So I set expectations as 'consistent category-relative edge with controlled tracking error,' not guaranteed alpha, and I add to Q2 starters (LAURUSLABS) only on flow confirmation to avoid premature drawdown.
📈 Technology & Digital Thematic · Fund Manager
balanced
11 peer funds · category median IR 0.21 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar ICICI Pru Technology Fund (G): IR 0.34, IC 0.023 (t 0.8), TE 18.2% — ahead but not yet significant
Benchmark NIFTY 500, category median IR 0.21. My honest read of my own skill stats: the desk's positive excess CAGR (+3.8 to +6.3%/yr across exemplars) sits on ic_mean of roughly 0 to +0.023 (t<1) — meaning the historical edge here is NOT per-name selection, it is the IT-theme beta and a few structural compounders. Fundamental Law verdict: with revealed IC near zero, I will not pretend stock-picking conviction I cannot defend. So the book is balanced, not risk-on: a quality-compounder core (Coforge, mid-cap IT) plus large-cap ballast (TechM), funded by trimming the structurally weak large-cap (Wipro), with a small digital-platform satellite. I keep tracking error in the desk-normal 16-18% band rather than chasing it higher, and I size ARM-led ideas small because ARM is a 0.03-0.045 IC, 1-6M tilt, not a guarantee.
Book tilt
Overweight mid-cap quality IT compounders (Coforge core, Hexaware/Mphasis starters) where ARM + fund flow independently confirm · Large-cap ballast via TechM only (the one large-cap where flow + revisions agree) to hold tracking error in the desk-normal 16-18% band · Underweight / source-of-funds: Wipro (fund exodus, flat earnings, stale ARM) · Small Digital satellite (Nazara) to express the '& Digital' mandate and de-correlate from IT-services beta · Selection sized humbly: revealed desk IC ~0, so breadth over concentration; ARM treated as a 1-6M tilt, not conviction
Pitches taken / passed
take COFORGE core from IT & Technology
Cleanest triple-confirm routed to my desk: top ARM + heavy fund accumulation + explosive PAT all align (Quadrant 1). This is the rare name where the thin-IC desk edge is reinforced by independent flow, so it earns core weight as the quality mid-cap compounder anchoring the sleeve.
take TECHM core from IT & Technology
Largest fund accumulation on the desk paired with high ARM and an earnings turnaround — the large-cap where smart money and the Street agree most. I need large-cap ballast to keep tracking error in my 16-18% band; this is the highest-conviction large-cap to anchor it rather than dead-weight index IT.
take HEXT starter from IT & Technology
Full Quadrant-1 mid-cap IT (analysts upgrading, funds buying, double-digit growth). Real confluence, but it overlaps Coforge in the same mid-cap-quality factor, so I size it starter to add breadth without doubling the same bet — correlated bets shrink effective breadth.
take MPHASIS starter from IT & Technology
Mid-cap Quadrant-1 confirmer (analyst revision + steady inflow + rising FII). Starter size to round out the mid-cap quality basket alongside Coforge/Hexaware and lift breadth, given my IC is too thin to concentrate.
take NAZARA starter from Telecom & Media
Digital/gaming platform tagged Technology & Digital — a genuine Quadrant-1 (analysts + funds both moving) backed by real earnings, not just momentum. Fits the '& Digital' half of my mandate and de-correlates the book from pure IT-services beta. Small-cap, so starter only.
pass WIPRO
Routed as a REDUCE and I agree — funds dumping at scale with flat earnings; the still-decent ARM looks stale against the exodus. I treat it as my source of funds to finance Coforge/TechM, not a holding. Declining to hold = taking the trim.
pass POLICYBZR
Fintech is tech-tagged and Quadrant-1, but it is a higher-beta financials expression flagged for risk-on desks. With my revealed IC near zero I do not add high-beta single-name bets I cannot defend on selection; it also stretches my mandate toward financials. Pass for now, would revisit on a momentum sleeve.
pass ICICIBANK
Strong Quadrant-1 pitch but Banking & Financials is out of my Technology & Digital mandate — not my book to own.
pass TITAN
Clean Quadrant-1 large-cap but Consumption mandate, not Technology & Digital. Out of scope.
Escalated to CIO
conflict WIPRO sizing as source-of-funds — Pitch 5 routes Wipro to Value & Contra (as a contra/value candidate) AND to CIO, while I want to use it as a funding trim. Confirm I can take it to underweight/zero in the tech sleeve before the Value desk establishes a contra position, so we are not net-buying and net-selling the same name across desks.
Investor experience
Consistency-first because my desk's honest edge is thin (ic_t<1, exemplar hit rate ~54-56%). Coforge/TechM/Hexaware/Mphasis are flow-AND-revision confirmed Quadrant-1 names, which keeps the path closer to the IT theme rather than a single idiosyncratic bet that can crater. Funding the adds by trimming Wipro reduces a known drawdown source (the fund exodus) rather than adding net risk. The Nazara satellite is sized small (small-cap, speculative) so a single digital name cannot dominate drawdown. Net: I am trading a little upside for a steadier, sittable path — appropriate when revealed selection skill is unproven and tracking error is already structurally high.
📈 Consumption Thematic · Fund Manager
balanced
25 peer funds · category median IR 0.21 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Mirae Asset Great Consumer Fund (G): IR 0.6, IC 0.037 (t 2.4), TE 6.5% — skilled
Focused consumption book, overweight Quadrant-1 quality compounders where analysts AND funds both confirm (TITAN, TRENT, TVSMOTOR, PHOENIXLTD), funded by avoiding flow-divergent / earnings-flat names. TE held inside my exemplar band (~5.0-6.5 vs the 25 consumption funds benchmarked to NIFTY 500), with TATACONSUM as a defensive FMCG anchor and PHOENIXLTD as a lower-beta annuity diversifier to keep the path sittable. ARM (IC 0.03-0.045, 1-6M clock) is used to SIZE positions, never to create them; I refuse the high-IR/zero-IC trap my Canara Robeco exemplar shows (IR 0.52 on IC 0.003, t 0.2 = no real selection edge).
Book tilt
Overweight Quadrant-1 quality consumption compounders (analysts + funds both positive) · Discretionary > staples, but anchored by a defensive TATACONSUM FMCG sleeve · Large / large-&-mid-cap core plus one lower-beta annuity diversifier (PHOENIXLTD) · ARM used purely as a position-sizing tilt (1-6M clock), never as position creation · Tracking error held in the ~5.0-6.5 exemplar band; avoid flow-divergent names
Pitches taken / passed
take TITAN core from Consumer
Cleanest Q1 large-cap discretionary compounder; analyst revisions, fund flow, earnings all aligned with no signal conflict (3-6M clock). The low-TE, positive-IC anchor my category-relative IR is built on.
take TRENT core from Consumer
Strongest flow-confirmed retail growth in coverage: funds accumulating hard AND analysts upgrading together - a both-forces setup at scale, not an ARM-only print, so the small ARM tilt is reinforced by money.
take TVSMOTOR core from Auto & Mobility
Explicitly Consumption-tagged; the only Q1 Auto name where ARM and fund flow both point up and earnings confirm (premium 2W/EV mix). A clean cross-sector consumption expression where I am NOT fighting the tape.
take PHOENIXLTD core from Realty & Infrastructure
Consumption-tagged annuity mall play: recurring rental cash flow, clean Q1, largest FII step-up in its desk, lower beta than pure developers - sized as a path/drawdown diversifier, not a beta chase.
take TATACONSUM starter from Consumer
Defensive sleeve: Q1 FMCG turnaround with best earnings growth among large-cap staples, analysts and funds both onside, and no distribution overhang vs Nestle/Asian Paints. Buys me drawdown ballast for a sittable path.
take VBL starter from Consumer
Highest-ARM large discretionary staple with steady double-digit earnings and supportive flow. Smaller size because the edge here leans on the ARM tilt (a small 0.03-0.045 IC signal) more than a both-forces scale move.
pass APOLLOHOSP
Quality Q1 name but it is the Pharma desk's hospital/healthcare-delivery thesis; taking it double-counts a Pharma-desk call. Decline on mandate cleanliness, not on quality.
pass EICHERMOT
-Rs339cr fund outflow, Quadrant-2 'analysts ahead' with funds NOT confirming. The 'profit-taking not a crack' read is a hope, not a force; on a 1-6M clock I want flow confirmation before committing capital.
pass HONASA
Micro-cap with a 'speculative ARM print' (analyst's own words) and low conviction - exactly the IC-illusion / sizing-drag trap (my ABSL exemplar lost -7% to bad sizing). A momentum-sleeve call, not a discretionary consumption book holding.
pass SAREGAMA
Reasonable music-IP annuity but small/mid with only modest fund flow; in a focused book with a finite TE budget it does not earn a slot over my Q1 cores.
Investor experience
Consistency over torque: my exemplars show high IR comes from a low-TE, positive-IC book (Mirae IR 0.60 / IC-t 2.4), not from chasing single-print momentum (Canara Robeco's IR 0.52 on IC ~0 is NOT selection skill). I deliberately weight two lower-beta sleeves - TATACONSUM (defensive FMCG) and PHOENIXLTD (annuity rentals) - to keep the lived drawdown shallow and the path sittable, and I avoid the flow-divergent / micro-cap names (EICHERMOT outflow, HONASA micro-cap) as much for drawdown control as for edge. Target: beat the 0.21 category-median IR consistently with a quality, mandate-clean book.
📈 Infrastructure Thematic · Fund Manager
balanced
19 peer funds · category median IR 0.52 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Invesco India Infrastructure Fund (G): IR 0.72, IC -0.013 (t -0.9), TE 10.5% — skilled
Constructive on the capex/infra theme vs NIFTY 500 (my benchmark), but NOT via index weight. My exemplars are the tell: Invesco IR 0.72 / Canara 0.61 / BoI 0.60 all clear the desk median IR 0.52, yet their stock-selection IC is ~zero-to-negative (Invesco -0.013 t-0.9; BoI -0.002 t-0.1; only Canara +0.02 t1.2) -- so infra alpha is a THEME bet vs the broad market, not per-name selection (IR=IC*sqrt(BR)*TC dominated by the theme tilt). I therefore concentrate the theme into its live earnings-inflection corners and diversify ACROSS sub-themes for breadth: OVERWEIGHT solar/renewables capex (Premier, Waaree, Solar Inds), defence (HAL), and Q1 real estate (Prestige residential + Phoenix annuity, de-correlated); UNDERWEIGHT the bellwether LT (Q4: no upward revisions, funds net sellers) rather than holding it on index heft alone. ARM (IC ~0.03-0.045, 1-6M clock) is used only as a within-theme tilt, never a per-name guarantee. TE budget (exemplars run 9.2-10.5%) is spent on the theme, not on punts.
Book tilt
Overweight renewables/solar capex (Premier, Waaree, Solar Inds) -- the live earnings-inflection corner of the theme · Overweight defence (HAL) -- flow+revisions agree, de-correlates from solar · Overweight Q1 real estate, split residential (Prestige) + annuity (Phoenix) for independent breadth · Underweight the bellwether LT (Q4) -- not held on index heft alone · Quality/liquidity discipline: small-cap renewables capped at core/starter despite high conviction; ARM used only as a within-theme tilt
Pitches taken / passed
take SOLARINDS core from Metals & Materials desk
Tagged Infrastructure; explosives/defence-linked structural compounder, not a pure commodity cyclical. Q1 (high ARM + fund accumulation + growing earnings), 3-6M clock fits my horizon. Larger and more liquid than the pure solar manufacturers, so it carries core weight as the quality anchor of my renewables/defence theme.
take HAL core from Industrials & Capital Goods desk
Defence megacap where flow AND revisions agree (both forces positive) -- the rare Q1 where ARM is reinforced by smart-money rather than fighting it. Large enough to size in a core thematic book ahead of the order cycle; anchors the defence sub-theme and de-correlates from solar.
take PREMIERENE core from Industrials & Capital Goods desk
Cleanest Q1 triple-confirm in solar manufacturing (ARM + MF buying + earnings all firing, 3-6M). Held to core not overweight despite high conviction: it is mid/small-cap with real liquidity and single-name concentration risk, and it shares the solar cycle with Waaree/Solar (correlated breadth), so I cap the single-name bet.
take WAAREEENER starter from Industrials & Capital Goods desk
Best growth+revision in renewables, Q1, funds still net buyers. But it rides the SAME solar-manufacturing cycle as Premier and Solar Inds -- correlated, so it shrinks effective breadth (sqrt(BR)). Sized starter so the three solar names together stay a controlled overweight, not a stacked single-factor bet.
take PRESTIGE core from Realty & Infrastructure desk
Cleanest Q1 in real estate (ARM + MF accumulation + earnings aligned, 3-6M) -- the highest-quality way to own the residential-cycle upturn at scale. De-correlated from the solar/defence cluster, so it adds genuine independent breadth to the book rather than doubling the same factor.
take PHOENIXLTD starter from Realty & Infrastructure desk
Annuity/mall play -- recurring rental cash flow, lower beta than residential developers, clean Q1 with the largest FII step-up in coverage. A defensive ballast inside a high-TE thematic book that improves the investor PATH (drawdown), sized starter as a stabiliser alongside the residential Prestige position.
pass LODHA
Q2 (analysts ahead, FII buying, but MF flow has NOT confirmed). Given ARM's small IC (~0.03-0.045), I want flow confirmation before paying for a large-cap entry; I already own the residential cycle via Prestige (Q1, both forces). Watch, not add yet.
pass DLF
HOLD benchmark weight only. Funds accumulating but ARM and earnings have not confirmed -- flow into a flat-earnings, mid-ARM megacap can be index-driven, not conviction. No ARM tilt to lean on, so no active add.
pass GODREJPROP
Clear Q4 -- analysts cutting AND funds selling; the large FII long looks like a crowded position unwinding. Avoid; both independent forces are negative, the opposite of what I want to own.
pass HFCL
Q3 (funds ahead, ARM not yet confirmed) and small-cap. +89% PAT is interesting but I will not pay for a telecom-equipment turnaround on flow alone with the analyst signal unconfirmed; track for ARM confirmation. Outside my core capex theme anyway.
Escalated to CIO
conflict LT REDUCE -- benchmark-heft underweight — LT is Q4 (no upward revisions, MF net sellers) yet it anchors every infra/large-cap index and is a heavy NIFTY 500 constituent. I want to run a meaningful UNDERWEIGHT, not hold on index weight -- but the size of that active short is a tracking-error/mandate call. How far below benchmark on LT is acceptable given my TE budget? Routed to CIO per the analyst flag.
risk-limit Solar-manufacturing concentration (Premier + Waaree + Solar Inds) — All three top renewables names ride ONE correlated solar-capex cycle, so combined they are a single-factor overweight that shrinks effective breadth (sqrt(BR)) and concentrates cycle/policy risk. I have self-capped (Premier+Solar core, Waaree starter), but request a CIO sign-off on a desk-level cap for the solar cluster so the book is not unknowingly stacked on one theme.
Investor experience
Honest holdability framing: my exemplars carry 9.2-10.5% tracking error and their alpha is a THEME bet vs NIFTY 500 (IC ~0, hit-rates only 52-60%), so this book WILL have stretches of theme-driven drawdown that index-hugging will not -- that is the cost of the mandate, not a defect. I manage the PATH three ways: (1) diversify across UNCORRELATED sub-themes (solar / defence / residential / annuity-realty) rather than stacking one cycle, so a single policy or capex shock does not sink the whole book; (2) include lower-beta annuity ballast (Phoenix rental cash flow) and a larger-cap quality anchor (Solar Inds, HAL) to soften the small-cap renewables volatility; (3) cap small-cap single-name weight even at high conviction. The target is a smoother ride to category-relative outperformance (beat desk median IR 0.52), not chasing the highest-torque small-caps -- consistency over a hero year.
📈 Balanced Advantage Hybrid & Asset Allocation · Fund Manager
balanced
36 peer funds · category median IR 0.28 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar SBI Balanced Advantage Fund (G): IR 0.97, IC 0.012 (t 0.7), TE 3.2% — skilled
Benchmark = NIFTY 500; my category median IR is only 0.28 and the three "skilled" exemplars post IR 0.76-0.97 on near-zero stock-selection IC (0.007-0.023, t 0.6-1.3) — Fundamental Law says a BAF's edge is the net-equity/asset-allocation lever and broad low-TE construction, NOT single-name bets. So I run a BENCHMARK-SHAPED book: tracking error targeted ~2.5-3.0% (between Mirae's 2.0 and SBI/ICICI's ~3.1), large-cap-tilted and quality-led, with the equity sleeve at a neutral-to-slightly-cautious net (consistent with "balanced" stance and good drawdown experience). Selection tilts come ONLY from clean Quadrant-1, large/large-mid names where ARM (0.03-0.045 IC, 1-6M clock) AND active fund flow agree — each is a small overweight (10-50bps active), never a concentrated swing. I decline single-name, small-cap, analyst-ahead, and divergence calls: my mandate is consistency and a sittable path, not torque.
Book tilt
Large-cap & quality lead; mid/small-cap single names deliberately excluded to keep tracking error ~2.5-3.0% · Overweight private financials (ICICIBANK) and quality consumption (TITAN) where Q1 both-forces-agree is cleanest · Underweight flagged-divergence heavyweights (VEDL, LT) but NOT zero — capped active underweights to protect TE · Selection tilts kept to 10-50bps each; the book's real risk lever is net-equity/allocation, consistent with the category's near-zero stock-selection IC · Net equity neutral-to-slightly-cautious for drawdown control — balanced stance, experience over torque
Pitches taken / passed
take ICICIBANK core from Banking & Financials
Large-cap Quadrant-1, heaviest active fund accumulation in the sector + supportive ARM, huge benchmark weight in NIFTY 500. Core private-bank holding I add INTO not chase; low idiosyncratic risk, both forces agree — exactly the profile that fits a low-TE balanced book.
take TITAN core from Consumer
Cleanest large-cap discretionary compounder, Quadrant-1 with no signal conflict (ARM + flow + earnings aligned), 3-6M clock. Quality large-cap consumption anchor; benchmark-shaped overweight, defensible on all three vectors.
take TVSMOTOR core from Auto & Mobility
The only Quadrant-1 in Auto where ARM and fund flow both point up while the rest of the sector sees trimming; large/large-mid, earnings confirming. A clean both-forces-agree large name to hold benchmark-plus.
take BHARTIARTL core from Telecom & Media
Sector anchor, large benchmark weight, overwhelming smart-money flow (+Rs8.5k cr/3m) and rising ARM. I hold it near benchmark weight for the flow/franchise, NOT as an ARM swing; I want the optical PAT delta checked before sizing ABOVE benchmark, so core not overweight.
take HINDZINC starter from Metals & Materials
Cleanest Q1 large-cap in metals — ARM, flow and earnings all agree. Small starter for diversified cyclical exposure; metals is volatile so I keep it a starter tilt, not core, to protect the experience mandate.
take ONGC starter from Energy & Power
Rare large-cap Quadrant-1 in energy: maxed ARM, accelerating earnings, funds already net buyers. Small starter overweight for energy representation where the small ARM tilt is reinforced by flow rather than fighting it.
pass COFORGE
Strong triple-confirm but mid-cap; single mid-cap names add idiosyncratic tracking error my low-TE consistency mandate can't justify. Mid-cap IT belongs on the mid-cap/tech desks, not a benchmark-shaped balanced book.
pass HEXT
Same reason as Coforge — mid-cap IT. Good Q1 setup but too small/idiosyncratic for a balanced book where IC is near-zero and the edge is allocation, not single mid-caps.
pass ANTHEM
Mid-cap CDMO; clean Q1 but a single mid-cap idea is the wrong risk shape for me. I get pharma via index weight and large-caps, not a concentrated CDMO bet.
pass BHARATFORG
Explicitly analyst-led with funds net SELLERS (Quadrant 2/3). ARM IC is only 0.03-0.045 — an unconfirmed revision tilt is exactly the kind of low-transfer bet that won't survive in a low-TE book. Pass until flow turns.
Escalated to CIO
risk-limit VEDL — Desk flags ARM 70 against -Rs13,051 cr MF distribution (sector's largest, order-of-magnitude outlier, possible de-merger/overhang). NIFTY 500 carries VEDL — confirm I should hold UNDERWEIGHT vs benchmark (not zero, to cap tracking error) until flow reverses; want CIO sign-off on the active underweight size given the flagged event risk.
conflict LT — Quadrant-4 warning (no upward revisions, funds net sellers) on a top benchmark constituent that anchors every infra/large-cap index. Holding it underweight is a meaningful active bet against a heavyweight; seek CIO guidance on how far below benchmark weight I may go without breaching my TE budget.
Investor experience
Mandate is CONSISTENT category-relative outperformance with a sittable path, not maximum return. My category's exemplars earn their IR (0.76-0.97) from allocation and broad construction on essentially zero selection IC, so I refuse to chase single-name torque (mid/small-caps, analyst-ahead, divergences) that would spike tracking error and drawdown. Targeted TE ~2.5-3.0% and a hit-rate goal near the exemplars' 56-64% — small, diversified, both-forces-confirmed large-cap tilts that compound quietly. The net-equity de-risking lever (the BAF's true edge) stays slightly cautious to limit downside capture and keep the rolling-positive experience smooth.
📈 Aggressive Hybrid Hybrid & Asset Allocation · Fund Manager
balanced
30 peer funds · category median IR 0.57 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Mahindra Manulife Aggressive Hybrid Fund-Reg (G): IR 1.04, IC -0.037 (t -2.0), TE 4.5% — skilled
Equity sleeve ~72% (within the aggressive-hybrid 65-80% band), ~22% debt/G-sec, ~6% arbitrage/cash as dry powder — deliberately NOT max equity, because my category edge (ICICI Pru template: IR 0.95, IC only +0.02, hit-rate 63.9%) is asset-allocation + breadth, not single-name heroics. The equity book is built from Quadrant-1 triple-confirm names (analysts upgrading AND funds buying AND earnings), sized small per the Fundamental Law (ARM IC ~0.03-0.045 is a tilt, not a guarantee). Vs NIFTY 500: modest overweight financials (ICICIBANK), consumption (TITAN/TRENT), and one IT mid-cap (COFORGE); underweight the contentious cyclical/forward-bet names. Target tracking error ~4-5% (between Mahindra Manulife's 4.5 and ICICI Pru's 6.3) — TE kept controlled because my exemplars show the IR comes from the book's balance, not from chasing dispersion. Many ~12-20 small Q1 positions for breadth over a few big bets.
Book tilt
Overweight confirmed Q1 financials (ICICIBANK core) — liquid, low-idiosyncratic · Consumption quality tilt (TITAN core + TRENT starter) for lower-beta compounding · One conviction mid-cap IT (COFORGE) instead of two — avoid doubling mid-cap risk · Underweight forward-bets / negative-trailing-PAT turnarounds and analyst-led-only (Q2) names · ~22% debt + ~6% arbitrage/cash held as a deliberate drawdown buffer, not maxed equity
Pitches taken / passed
take ICICIBANK core from Banking & Financials
Cleanest large-cap bank, Quadrant 1 (analysts+funds both positive), heaviest active fund accumulation in the sector with supportive ARM. A liquid, low-idiosyncratic-risk core holding — the kind of broad, well-confirmed tilt that builds IR through balance not single-name bets. Anchors the equity sleeve.
take TITAN core from Consumer
Q1 large-cap discretionary compounder, all three signals (revisions, flow, earnings) aligned with no conflict, 3-6M clock. Quality consumption holding that lowers book beta vs pure cyclicals — supports the good-experience / drawdown-control mandate.
take COFORGE core from IT & Technology
The desk's cleanest triple-confirm: top ARM + heavy fund accumulation + explosive earnings. My one conviction mid-cap IT expression; gives the book IT exposure without leaning on the slow-growth large-cap majors. Liquid enough for a hybrid; sized core not overweight given mid-cap volatility.
take TRENT starter from Consumer
Strongest flow-confirmed retail growth, funds accumulating hard with analysts upgrading — a rare both-positive at scale. Higher-beta than Titan so sized starter; pairs with Titan for a confirmed consumption tilt that is the steadiest Q1 cluster on the board.
take TVSMOTOR starter from Auto & Mobility
The only Quadrant-1 name in Auto — analysts AND funds both adding while the rest of the sector sees funds trimming. Premiumisation showing in earnings. A clean confirmed way to own auto without the analyst-led-only (BHARATFORG) or flow-led-only (MARUTI) traps. Sized starter for sector breadth.
take HAL starter from Industrials & Capital Goods
Defence megacap where flow AND revisions agree, liquid enough to size in a core hybrid book unlike the small-cap infra names. Structural order-cycle exposure with large-cap liquidity — fits a hybrid better than ENGINERSIN/PREMIERENE small-caps. Starter on a 3-6M clock.
pass HEXT
Good Q1 mid-cap IT, but I already get my IT conviction from COFORGE. Two mid-cap IT names would over-concentrate idiosyncratic mid-cap risk in a drawdown-sensitive hybrid book. Decline to avoid doubling the same bet.
pass POLICYBZR
Higher-beta fintech 'risk-on' expression — explicitly flagged for risk-on desks. As a balanced-stance aggressive hybrid prioritising investor experience, I don't want speculative-beta financials when ICICIBANK already gives me a clean, liquid Q1 financials core.
pass GUJGASLTD
Explicitly a FORWARD bet with trailing PAT still negative, routed to CIO by the analyst. A turnaround on negative trailing earnings is the opposite of the consistent, low-drawdown quality book my mandate demands. Decline.
pass BHARATFORG
Highest ARM on the desk but funds are net sellers (Quadrant 2, analyst-led only). The analyst explicitly says discretionary desks should wait for flow; ARM's IC alone (~0.03-0.045) is too thin to override a flow divergence in a hybrid. Pass until flow turns.
Escalated to CIO
conflict VEDL — ARM 70 but funds dumped -13,051 cr (sector's largest distribution by an order of magnitude) — I hold no VEDL and won't add, but this de-merger/overhang signal is large enough that I want a firm-wide read: is the CIO marking down a structural risk the Street ARM hasn't caught yet, and should other desks be capped on it?
mandate Equity sleeve target — running ~72% equity vs a band that allows up to 80% — Confirm I can keep ~6-8% in arbitrage/cash as drawdown dry-powder rather than maxing equity; my exemplars (ICICI Pru IR 0.95) earn IR from allocation balance, and I'd rather hold optionality than be fully invested into a thin-breadth board.
Investor experience
Mandate is consistent category-relative outperformance with a GOOD investor experience, so the book is built for path, not peak. I deliberately run ~72% equity (not 80%) and hold ~6% arbitrage/cash for drawdown buffer — my exemplar ICICI Pru Equity & Debt earns IR 0.95 at hit-rate 63.9% from balance, not from being fully invested. Equity names are many small Q1 positions (breadth lowers single-name blow-up risk; per the Fundamental Law, IR builds from breadth × transfer, and ARM is only a ~0.03-0.045 IC tilt). I tilt to lower-beta quality (TITAN, ICICIBANK, HAL) over speculative beta (declined POLICYBZR, GUJGAS turnaround) to keep rolling drawdown shallow and the experience sittable. I respect TE (~4-5%) so the fund tracks NIFTY 500 closely enough that a nervous investor stays invested — consistency over hero quarters.
📈 Multi-Asset Hybrid & Asset Allocation · Fund Manager
balanced
34 peer funds · category median IR 0.27 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Nippon India Multi Asset Allocation Fund-Reg (G): IR 1.27, IC -0.017 (t -0.8), TE 2.9% — skilled
Benchmark NIFTY 500, category median IR a modest 0.27. My exemplars (Nippon IR 1.27, Tata 1.05) earn that IR with near-ZERO stock-picking IC (-0.017, +0.014, t<1) and tight tracking error (2.9-3.0) - their edge is ASSET ALLOCATION + sizing discipline, not hero names; Quant (IR 1.0, TE 17.5) is the high-octane outlier I will NOT imitate. So I run a balanced, large-cap-anchored equity sleeve tracking the benchmark closely (target TE ~3, not 17), use ARM's small ~0.03-0.045 IC only to TILT within the sleeve toward Quadrant-1 quality where funds confirm analysts, and spread across 7-8 sectors for de-correlated breadth. Modest overweight to financials/consumer/quality-IT, underweight the speculative small-caps and force-divergence traps.
Book tilt
Large-cap-anchored equity sleeve tracking NIFTY 500 closely (target TE ~3, NOT the 17.5 octane route) · Overweight Quadrant-1 quality where funds confirm analysts; zero tolerance for Q3 chase-traps and force divergences · Sector breadth across financials, consumer, auto, IT, telecom, metals for de-correlated bets (breadth, not concentration) · Quality mid-cap tilt kept to starter sizing (Coforge) so the equity sleeve stays low-TE · ARM used only as a small ~0.04-IC tilt on a 1-6M clock, never as a per-name guarantee or a sizing driver
Pitches taken / passed
take ICICIBANK core from Banking & Financials
Cleanest large-cap bank, Quadrant-1, heaviest active fund accumulation in the sector with supportive ARM, 3-6M clock. Highest-quality benchmark-aligned anchor - exactly the low-TE quality core my exemplars built IR on.
take TITAN core from Consumer
Clean Q1 large-cap discretionary compounder - analyst revisions, flow and earnings all aligned, no signal conflict. Defensive-growth ballast for a good investor experience.
take TVSMOTOR core from Auto & Mobility
Only Quadrant-1 name in Auto - both forces up while the rest of the sector sees funds trimming; earnings confirming the premiumisation/EV mix. Large-cap quality, the rare confirmed name.
take BHARTIARTL core from Telecom & Media
Sector anchor, overwhelming smart-money flow (+Rs8.5k cr/3m) and rising ARM, strongest FII build in coverage. Large-cap defensive-growth; I size to core but flag the optical/soft headline PAT to verify before adding further.
take COFORGE starter from IT & Technology
Cleanest triple-confirm on the IT desk - top ARM, heavy fund accumulation, explosive earnings, Q1. Best name to express a quality mid-cap IT tilt; sized starter not core because mid-cap raises my tracking error vs a NIFTY 500 sleeve.
take HINDZINC starter from Metals & Materials
Cleanest Q1 large-cap metal - ARM, fund buying and earnings all point up, the two independent signals confirm not fight. A measured commodity-cyclical diversifier that de-correlates the financials/consumer core; starter size to keep cyclical risk contained.
pass VEDL
Loudest force conflict on the board: ARM 70 but mutual funds dumped -13,051 cr, the sector's largest distribution by an order of magnitude. Flow that large against a decent ARM signals de-merger/overhang risk the Street has not marked - not addable until flow reverses. Escalated, not bought.
pass HINDPETRO
Quadrant-3 trap - heavy fund buying and big reported PAT but ARM at the floor (2). The Street is fading the OMC profit spike as a marketing-margin windfall; I side with revisions over the chase. Off-mandate octane for a balanced book.
pass HONASA
Micro-cap with a speculative ARM print; the analyst itself says size only on a momentum sleeve. Too illiquid and high-variance for a low-TE Multi-Asset book.
pass MARUTI
Flow running ahead of the signal - ARM sub-50 and flat earnings. Fund conviction without ARM backing is not an edge I will lead with; benchmark weight only.
Escalated to CIO
mandate LT (Larsen & Toubro) - REDUCE on a Q4 signal — LT is the infra/large-cap bellwether anchoring NIFTY 500; analyst flags Q4 (no upward revision, funds net sellers). Underweighting my benchmark heavyweight is a tracking-error/mandate call above my desk - confirm how far below index weight I may go before it breaches the TE band.
conflict VEDL - ARM 70 vs -13,051 cr fund distribution — Genuine, order-of-magnitude force conflict already routed to CIO. Ruling: do we treat the record outflow as the leading signal (de-merger/overhang) and stay out across all desks, or is there a contra case? I recommend OUT until flow reverses.
large-size LICI - large-cap Q2 divergence (analysts ahead, funds selling) — Highest ARM in its coverage but funds net sellers and FII flat in a benchmark-heavyweight insurer; analyst routed to CIO. Ask: do we want any starter exposure on ARM alone given its index heft, or wait for flow confirmation? I prefer waiting.
Investor experience
My IR comes from allocation discipline and a low-drawdown path, not hero names - the exemplars prove it (Nippon/Tata earn IR 1.0-1.3 at TE ~3 with ~0 stock IC; hit rates 53-63%). So I keep the equity sleeve large-cap-heavy and benchmark-close for a smooth ride, take six confirmed Q1 names spread across sectors so no single bet drives the experience, and refuse the small-cap/force-divergence trades that create gap-risk drawdowns. The asset-allocation overlay (equity/debt/gold) - not single stocks - is my main consistency and downside-control lever; the ARM tilt is a minor return enhancer on a 1-6M clock, expected to add a little, never to rescue a path.
📈 Equity Savings Hybrid & Asset Allocation · Fund Manager
defensive
25 peer funds · category median IR 0.14 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar Mirae Asset Equity Savings Fund (G): IR 0.87, IC 0.019 (t 1.4), TE 2.8% — skilled
Equity Savings is a low-volatility hybrid: ~20-35% net directional equity, the rest arbitrage/hedged + debt, so the lived path (shallow drawdown, smooth bond-plus return) IS the product, not index beta. My benchmark is NIFTY 500 and category median IR is only 0.14 — the genuinely skilled peers (Mirae IR 0.87 on TE 2.8, IC 0.019; PGIM IR 0.57) win by MANY small, well-implemented bets inside a TIGHT tracking-error budget, not hero picks. Per the Fundamental Law (IR = IC x sqrt(breadth) x transfer), their high IR off a small IC means the edge is breadth + transfer, i.e. staying close to benchmark and tilting gently. So I run the directional sleeve LARGE-CAP, QUALITY, Quadrant-1, low-beta, keeping TE near 2.5-3.5; I treat ARM only as a 1-6M tilt (IC ~0.03-0.045), never a thesis; and I lean on the desk's REDUCE/AVOID flags to stay underweight deteriorating index names, which protects the drawdown path.
Book tilt
Large-cap quality, low-beta Quadrant-1 only; ARM used as a 1-6M tilt, never a thesis · Tracking error held tight (~2.5-3.5) vs NIFTY 500 — breadth + transfer over hero bets, per the skilled-peer template · Financials/large-cap-bank core (ICICIBANK) + franchise anchors (RELIANCE, BHARTIARTL) · Underweight deteriorating index names via desk AVOID/REDUCE flags (WIPRO, BIOCON, VEDL, LT, HINDPETRO, ZEEL) · No small/mid-cap torque, no Quadrant-2 unconfirmed names, no high-beta risk-on expressions
Pitches taken / passed
take ICICIBANK core from Banking & Financials
Cleanest large-cap Q1 bank: heaviest active fund accumulation in the sector + supportive ARM, low-beta core private bank. Exactly the kind of low-TE quality anchor my book needs.
take RELIANCE core from Energy & Power
Large-cap franchise anchor near benchmark weight with the desk's largest fund accumulation and solid earnings; ARM only neutral so I own it for flow/franchise, not the tilt — a low-TE index-anchor holding.
take BHARTIARTL core from Telecom & Media
Defensive large-cap annuity with overwhelming smart-money flow and rising revisions; soft headline PAT is the only caveat so I hold core not overweight pending the PAT-delta check.
take TITAN starter from Consumer
Clean large-cap discretionary compounder, Q1 with no signal conflict; quality tilt but consumption is richly valued so I start small.
take ONGC starter from Energy & Power
Rare large-cap Q1 value: ARM maxed, earnings accelerating, funds already net buyers; cheap, dividend-supported, low-beta — a defensible value tilt for a savings book sized for caution given it is a cyclical.
take APOLLOHOSP starter from Pharma & Healthcare
Best-aligned large-cap on the pharma desk, every signal positive, structural healthcare-delivery theme with no PAT/flow blemish; rich valuation and 3-6M clock so a quality starter only.
pass COFORGE
Mid-cap IT torque; too much beta/drawdown risk for a low-vol savings book despite the clean triple-confirm.
pass HINDZINC
Q1 but a commodity cyclical — wrong volatility profile for a hybrid whose product is a smooth path; metals belong in equity desks, not here.
pass LAURUSLABS
Mid-cap and Quadrant-2 (analysts ahead, funds not confirming) — unconfirmed flow plus mid-cap beta fail my low-TE quality bar.
pass TRENT
High-beta L&M retail growth; strong setup but the volatility and valuation are unsuited to the hedged/defensive directional sleeve.
Escalated to CIO
conflict VEDL — go to zero vs hold benchmark weight — Analyst flags immediate AVOID: ARM healthy (70) but MFs dumped -13,051 cr, the sector's largest distribution (de-merger/overhang). It is an index constituent I'd otherwise carry. Take it to zero weight, or hold a token benchmark weight until flow stabilises?
large-size ICICIBANK single-name cap — It is my largest intended directional position. For a hybrid with a small net-equity sleeve, confirm the single-name cap (I'm sizing it as core, ~top of the band) before I add further on the heavy fund accumulation.
Investor experience
Priority is a shallow, sittable path: directional sleeve kept small (~25-30% net long), large-cap and low-beta, with the rest in arbitrage/debt so single-name shocks are muted. By skewing to Quadrant-1 quality and using the AVOID flags to dodge the names that are cracking (VEDL flow blow-up, BIOCON earnings collapse, ZEEL), I cut left-tail risk to the holder's lived experience. Target the skilled-peer profile — hit-rate above 55% and a consistent positive excess like Mirae's +2.6%/yr at TE 2.8 — rather than chasing category-leading return, because consistency and a shallow drawdown ARE the investor experience this fund is bought for.
📈 Quant / Systematic Quant · Fund Manager
balanced
42 peer funds · category median IR 0.44 · bench NIFTY 500
exemplar 360 ONE Quant Fund (G): IR 1.02, IC 0.029 (t 1.0), TE 6.5% — skilled
Diversified, ARM-tilted basket benchmarked to NIFTY 500 (desk median IR 0.44). My edge is NOT per-name conviction — revealed IC on the desk's own exemplars is statistically zero (360 ONE Quant IC 0.029/t=1.0; Quant Multi-Asset IC -0.004/t=-0.1), so their IR came from sector/allocation bets, not selection. Per the Fundamental Law (IR=IC*sqrt(BR)*TC), with ARM IC only ~0.03-0.045 and a 1-6M clock, I harvest the edge through BREADTH (many near-independent Quadrant-1 tilts across 6+ sectors) and TRANSFER (actually sizing the tilts), not concentration. Book runs a modest pro-cyclical tilt with deliberate liquid large-cap ballast (ICICIBANK, ONGC, HINDZINC, COFORGE) wrapped around higher-ARM small/mid tilts to keep tracking error near the ~6.5 exemplar level and the path sittable.
Book tilt
Quadrant-1 ARM+flow confluence is the core filter — analysts upgrading AND funds buying, so the small ~0.03-0.045 IC tilt is reinforced not fighting the tape · Breadth over conviction: 6+ near-independent sector tilts to lift effective BR, since per-name revealed IC is ~zero — the edge is the portfolio, not any one name · Liquid large-cap ballast (ONGC, ICICIBANK, HINDZINC, COFORGE) wrapped around higher-ARM small/mid tilts to keep tracking error and impact cost in band · Decline flow-only (Q3) and analyst-only-with-selling names — no confirmed ARM tilt means no systematic edge for this desk · Short-clock discipline: ARM is a 1-6M signal, so tilts are starters/cores to be re-ranked on the monthly refresh, not buy-and-forget
Pitches taken / passed
take ENGINERSIN starter from Industrials & Capital Goods
Highest ARM in the 143-name coverage WITH funds net buyers and double-digit PAT — a clean Quadrant-1 short-clock revision tilt routed to my desk; the breadth-additive systematic bet I want, but small-cap so sized small for liquidity.
take ONGC core from Energy & Power
Rare LARGE-CAP Quadrant-1: ARM maxed, earnings accelerating, funds already net buyers, routed to me. The small ARM tilt is reinforced by flow, and the liquidity de-risks the book — my best risk/reward and a core ballast.
take COFORGE core from IT & Technology
Cleanest triple-confirm on the IT desk — top ARM, heavy fund accumulation, explosive earnings, Quadrant-1. Liquid mid-cap quality the Street is actively upgrading; high breadth value, no offsetting flag.
take ICICIBANK core from Banking & Financials
Cleanest large-cap bank, Quadrant-1 with the heaviest active fund accumulation in the sector and supportive ARM. Adds an independent financials tilt and liquid ballast to offset the small/mid ARM names.
take HINDZINC core from Metals & Materials
Cleanest Q1 large-cap metals — ARM, fund buying and earnings align; two independent signals confirm. Adds a de-correlated commodity-cyclical sleeve to raise effective breadth.
take LAURUSLABS starter from Pharma & Healthcare
Routed to my desk; Quadrant-2 (analysts ahead, funds not yet chasing) with a real earnings inflection. ARM IC is actionable on a systematic momentum sleeve precisely before flow confirms — a starter tilt, not a chase.
pass BHARATFORG
Highest ARM on the desk but funds are net SELLERS (analyst-led, money not confirming). With IC only ~0.03-0.045 I want flow confirmation before sizing; watchlist, not a buy.
pass HONASA
Strongest revision momentum but micro-cap with a self-flagged 'speculative ARM print'. Liquidity/impact cost would eat the tilt — fails the transfer-coefficient test.
pass MRPL
Q1 and routed to me, but a higher-beta small-cap proxy for the SAME upstream/refining theme as ONGC, which I already own large-cap. Correlated bet adds little independent breadth; pass to avoid doubling the theme.
pass MARUTI
Self-flagged: ARM sub-50, earnings flat, flow-driven only. No ARM tilt means no systematic edge — fund conviction running ahead of the signal, not a quant bet.
Escalated to CIO
conflict VEDL — ARM 70 vs mutual funds dumping -13,051 cr (sector's largest distribution, an order of magnitude above peers) — Genuine force conflict at extreme scale: a healthy ARM against the loudest fund exodus on the metals desk likely signals de-merger/overhang risk the Street has not yet marked down. Do NOT add on ARM alone — confirm whether the flow reflects a structural event before any desk sizes it.
risk-limit Aggregate small/mid-cap ARM concentration (ENGINERSIN, LAURUSLABS plus any momentum-sleeve adds) — Confirm the desk cap-band/liquidity limit so the small/mid ARM tilt stays sized for impact cost and keeps tracking error near the ~6.5 exemplar level — I am holding these as starters precisely to respect that.
Investor experience
Targeting CONSISTENT, beat-the-0.44-median outperformance via many small independent tilts rather than a few big bets — this caps single-name blow-up risk and keeps the drawdown path sittable. Tracking error anchored near the exemplar ~6.5 by pairing high-ARM small/mids with liquid large-cap ballast; no single tilt dominates. Honest expectation-setting: ARM's revealed IC is small and short-clocked, so I expect a respectable, repeatable hit-rate (~58-62% like the exemplars) and steady relative compounding — NOT lottery alpha — with monthly re-ranking on the ARM refresh to control downside capture.
🏛️ Chief Investment Officer House view
The tape is a STOCK-PICKER'S market, not a beta market. No sector ARM clears 63 float-weighted; the unweighted median sector ARM sits near 50 (Industrials 44.5 EW is the weakest, the megacap LT/ABB/Siemens drag pulling float-weight to 39.2). Edge this cycle lives almost entirely in Quadrant 1 (analysts upgrading AND funds buying), and Q1 breadth is concentrated in four desks: Financials (39 Q1 names), Diversified (37), Consumer (36) and Healthcare (28). The Fundamental Law (IR = IC x sqrt(BR) x TC) is the firm's whole edge here: per-name ARM IC is only ~0.03-0.045 on a 1-6M clock, so we win by BREADTH of independent confirmed bets and by TRANSFER (actually sizing them), never by concentration or by lone-ARM hero calls. Float-weight ARM leading equal-weight (Technology 62.4>57.6, Consumer 52.8>47.7, Materials 53.4>47.2) tells us the quality/large end is where revisions concentrate inside otherwise middling sectors -- so the standing instruction across desks is: own confirmed quality at the large/quality end, harvest mid-cap Q1 quality where flow confirms, refuse single-force tilts. Firmwide directive: triple-confirm (ARM + flow + PAT) sized as breadth; treat every loud force-divergence (VEDL, LICI) as a risk event to manage, not an alpha to chase.
3-lens market pulse — the gaps between the lenses are the signal
Street · analysts / ARM
Analysts (ARM) are selectively constructive, never broadly bullish. The only sectors with ARM above the high-50s are Telecom EW 59.4, Technology FF 62.4, Healthcare EW 56.9 and Financials EW 55.2 -- and in every one the float-weight vs equal-weight split shows upgrades are CONCENTRATED (Tech FF 62.4 > EW 57.6 = large-cap quality; Telecom EW 59.4 > FF 55.9 = the edge is in smaller media/content names, NOT heavy-telecom incumbents). Highest individual ARM prints are revision outliers funds are NOT confirming: LICI 99, ONGC 98, NAZARA 98, Bharat Forge 84, VEDL 70.
Smart money · fund flows
Fund flows tell a partly DIFFERENT story than analysts. Funds are aggressively buying large index anchors regardless of revisions -- Maruti +Rs2,559cr and Hyundai +Rs1,811cr (weak Auto ARM 46.1), RELIANCE +Rs3,801cr (neutral ARM 49), BHARTIARTL +Rs8,521cr. They are DISTRIBUTING the loud-ARM names: VEDL -Rs13,051cr against ARM 70, LICI -Rs934cr against ARM 99, LUPIN -Rs1,975cr, GLAND -Rs579cr. So smart money is paying up for liquidity/franchise and selling crowded revision stories.
Reward · price / quadrant
Quadrant behaviour (price/positioning): the largest single bucket firmwide is Quadrant 4 (both weak) -- Diversified alone has 125 of 288 names in Q4, Industrials 40, Financials 25, Materials 23. Quadrant 3 (funds buying AHEAD of analysts) is next-biggest in Consumer (44), Industrials (42), Materials (25), Energy (15) -- flow front-running revisions in cyclicals. Q1 (the reward-and-confirmation zone) is a minority everywhere except Healthcare (28/68 = the cleanest confirm-breadth on the board).
★ The gaps
THE GAPS ARE THE SIGNAL. Three structural divergences: (1) VEDL -- street says ARM 70 (healthy), smart money says -Rs13,051cr (record exodus): a gap this large = an event analysts have not marked (de-merger/overhang); trust the flow. (2) The OEM/anchor gap -- funds buying Maruti/Hyundai/Reliance where ARM is neutral-weak: liquidity/franchise demand, NOT alpha, so hold benchmark-weight, do not tilt on it. (3) LICI/ZYDUSLIFE/Bharat Forge -- analysts ahead, funds absent (Q2): revision-only, IC too thin to size alone -- wait for flow. LEVEL agreement across forces = crowding (fragile); CHANGE agreement = conviction (Q1). The reward is in CHANGE-agreement, the risk is in the LEVEL-agreement crowds.
Allocation across mandates
Core Equity -- Large Cap (median IR 0.49), Multi Cap (0.71), Flexi Cap (0.48), Small Cap (0.45), Mid Cap (0.28), Large & Mid (0.24)OVERWEIGHT the division, led by Multi Cap and Flexi Cap.The firm's breadth engine; Multicap's 0.71 median IR is the highest category bar on the board -- exactly the breadth-x-transfer profile the tape rewards. Mandate the diversified Q1 confluence book: own confirmed quality (ICICIBANK, TITAN, BHARTIARTL core, COFORGE/HEXAWARE mid-cap quality) spread across 8+ sectors. Large & Mid (escalation 4): APPROVE spending to ~5% TE to hold the Coforge/Trent/Prestige alpha sleeve at conviction -- but cap any single correlated mid-cap-IT cluster; the TE budget buys breadth, not stacked correlated bets. Refuse lone-ARM and Q2 tilts firm-wide.
Strategy -- Value & Contra (median IR 0.60), ELSS (0.44), Dividend Yield (0.45), Focused (0.40)NEUTRAL-to-modest overweight; style discipline over torque.Value's 0.60 IR is strong, but the data shows these books earn IR from persistent STYLE TILT + hit-rate, not per-name selection IC (exemplar ICs ~0 to negative). Value runs upstream-energy/dividend-yield contrarian breadth (ONGC, NMDC, HINDZINC where ARM confirms but valuation has not re-rated), NOT the consensus momentum cluster; its one sanctioned divergence starter = LICI (tiny, scale on flow).
