Focused (escalation 15): single-name ICICIBANK at overweight to express financials is APPROVED within a ~4-5% TE Focused mandate -- concentration via conviction/transfer is this desk's design -- but no speculative small-cap ARM torque in a low-breadth book. Keep VEDL/WIPRO out of all Strategy books.
Thematic -- Infrastructure (median IR 0.52), Pharma (0.48), Banking (0.37), Consumption (0.21), Technology (0.21)SELECTIVE -- overweight Healthcare/Infra expression, underweight broad Tech/Consumption theme beta.Pharma desk has the cleanest confirm-breadth on the board (Healthcare 28/68 Q1) -- mandate the diversified CDMO+hospitals+large-pharma overweight (ANTHEM, LAURUS, APOLLOHOSP), avoid the triple-negative tail (BIOCON, COHANCE, PPLPHARMA) and heavy-distribution generics (LUPIN -1,975, GLAND -579). Infra (median IR 0.52, best Thematic) expresses the capex theme through live earnings-inflection corners (solar capped per ruling, HAL, Q1 realty) and runs LT underweight. Tech/Consumption thematic IRs are only 0.21 with near-zero revealed IC -- keep these books humble, breadth over concentration, ARM sized small.
Hybrid & Asset Allocation -- Aggressive Hybrid (median IR 0.57), Balanced Advantage (0.28), Multi-Asset (0.27), Equity Savings (0.14)NEUTRAL; defensive optionality APPROVED.These desks' edge is the net-equity/allocation lever, NOT stock selection (exemplar selection ICs ~0). Aggressive Hybrid (escalation 27): APPROVE ~72% equity with 6-8% arbitrage/cash dry-powder rather than maxing to 80% -- on a thin-breadth board optionality is right and consistent with the allocation-edge evidence. BAF/Multi-Asset: keep TE tight (~2.5-3.0), large-cap quality Q1 tilts at 10-50bps each, decline small-cap/Q2/divergence calls. Equity Savings (IR 0.14, lowest bar): large-cap quality, low-beta, lean on AVOID flags (WIPRO/VEDL/LT/BIOCON) to protect the shallow-drawdown path that IS the product. ICICIBANK single-name core cap approved (escalation 32).
Quant / Systematic (median IR 0.44)NEUTRAL -- the firm's pure breadth-harvest book.Revealed selection IC on this desk is statistically ~zero, so per the Fundamental Law the entire edge is breadth (many near-independent Q1 ARM+flow tilts across 6+ sectors) + transfer (sizing them) + liquid large-cap ballast (ONGC, ICICIBANK, HINDZINC, COFORGE) to hold TE near ~6.5. Escalation 34: APPROVE the small/mid ARM names (ENGINERSIN, LAURUSLABS) as STARTERS only, sized for impact cost -- cap-band/liquidity discipline keeps the tilt sittable. Decline flow-only (Q3) and analyst-only names: no confirmed ARM tilt = no systematic edge here. Re-rank monthly on the 1-6M clock; nothing buy-and-forget.
Rulings
  • VEDL -- ARM 70 vs -Rs13,051cr fund distribution (escalations 9,11,16,18,24,26,29,31,33 -- raised by ELEVEN desks): FIRM-WIDE STANCE: HOLD UNDERWEIGHT vs benchmark, DO NOT ADD, across every desk, until flow stabilises. NOT zero for benchmarked books (cap the active underweight to protect tracking error) -- but no discretionary weight anywhere. NOT a contra entry yet. — A -Rs13,051cr outflow is an order of magnitude larger than any other distribution on the board and dwarfs the +0.03-0.045 IC an ARM of 70 can carry. When flow and revisions diverge at THIS scale, flow is pricing a structural event (de-merger/overhang) the Street has not yet marked into estimates. Per the Fundamental Law a thin-IC analyst signal cannot overrule a record-scale flow event. Value & Contra: no contra entry until the de-merger structure is understood AND flow reverses -- cheapness here is event-risk, not a margin of safety.
  • LT -- Quadrant-4 benchmark bellwether underweight (escalations 7,10,17,22,25,28 -- raised by SIX desks): SANCTIONED ACTIVE UNDERWEIGHT, sized to mandate. Benchmarked NIFTY-500 books (Multicap, Flexi, ELSS, Multi-Asset, BAF): underweight up to ~half of index weight (NOT zero -- its heft would blow TE on a thin signal). Thematic Infrastructure desk, whose mandate IS the capex theme: may run a deeper underweight (toward zero of the active sleeve) because it expresses the theme through Premier/Waaree/HAL/Prestige instead. No desk is penalised for being below index weight on LT. — LT is Q4 (no upward revisions, funds net sellers) -- both forces agree on weakness, which IS a conviction signal to fade. But it anchors every infra/large-cap index, so an unbounded underweight is a large active bet funded by one Q4 read. We accept the underweight on signal but cap it by TE budget: the firm rewards the fade, it does not reward blowing the tracking-error mandate to express it.
  • BHARTIARTL -- headline PAT -9.8% vs +Rs8,521cr fund bid + rising ARM (escalations 2,8,14 -- Large Cap, Flexi, Focused): PAT caveat NOT independently verifiable from our data, so size on the CONFIRMED forces, not the unverified one. HOLD CORE on the flow + ARM + FII confluence (a clean smart-money + revisions + foreign-bid triple). Do NOT lift to overweight in concentrated/Focused books until the PAT delta is confirmed optical (one-off / below-the-line). Diversified large-cap books (Large Cap, Flexi) may hold core-to-modest-overweight now; the concentrated Focused book waits. — +Rs8,521cr is the single heaviest fund bid on the board and ARM is rising -- two independent forces confirming. A -9.8% HEADLINE PAT on a telecom with known exceptional/below-the-line items is most likely optical, but no desk sizes a top-5 slot on my assumption. The rule scales with breadth: a diversified book absorbs residual earnings risk; a low-breadth Focused book cannot absorb a single blow-up, so it waits for earnings-quality confirmation.
  • LICI -- ARM 99 (highest in coverage) vs -Rs934cr fund selling, FII flat, Q2 (escalations 1,6,12,19,30 -- raised by FIVE desks): WAIT FOR FLOW. No meaningful weight on revisions alone. A SMALL starter permitted ONLY in explicitly contra-mandated books (Value & Contra) as an early-divergence entry, scaled only on flow confirmation. All core/benchmark books (Large Cap, Multicap, Banking, Multi-Asset): benchmark-weight or below until MF flow turns positive. PAT +18.9% is supportive but does not override negative flow. — ARM 99 is the loudest revision on the board, but it is a Q2 divergence (analysts ahead, funds absent) and ARM's standalone IC ~0.03-0.045 is far too thin to carry a 565k-cr position against active fund SELLING. The asymmetry is wrong: if we are right we capture a thin tilt; if the funds are right we own a large name into distribution. Only a desk whose explicit edge is early-divergence contrarianism (Value) earns a starter, and only as a starter.
  • Solar-manufacturing cross-desk concentration -- Premier Energies + Waaree (+ Solar Inds) routed to multiple desks (escalations 5,23 -- Mid Cap, Infrastructure): FIRM-LEVEL CAP IMPOSED. Each name is individually Q1, but Premier + Waaree + Solar Inds ride ONE correlated solar-capex/policy cycle -- combined they are a single-factor bet that shrinks effective breadth. Mid Cap may DECLINE both at book level (correct -- protects TE). Infrastructure, whose mandate is the theme, may hold the cluster but capped: Premier + Solar core, Waaree starter, with TOTAL solar-cluster active weight capped so it is not the book's largest single-factor exposure. CIO tracks aggregate firm solar exposure across all desks. — Textbook crowding the Fundamental Law warns against: three names that LOOK like three independent bets share one driver and count as ~one bet for breadth. Each FM looks diversified while the FIRM accumulates concentrated cycle/policy risk. The thematic Infra desk is the right home for managed exposure; the diversified Mid Cap book is right to decline. Correlated thematic risk is capped at the firm level, not left to each desk's self-assessment.
  • WIPRO as cross-desk source-of-funds vs Value-desk contra candidate (escalations 13,21 -- Focused, Technology): WIPRO ARM is STALE, not early -- confirmed. Tech and Focused desks MAY trim/exit WIPRO to benchmark-underweight to fund COFORGE adds. The Value & Contra desk has explicitly classified WIPRO as a value-TRAP (hard avoid: cheapness masking a deteriorating case), so there is NO cross-desk conflict -- we are not net-buying and net-selling the same name. Net firm stance: underweight WIPRO. Proceed. — WIPRO shows a stale-decent ARM against a large MF exodus and flat earnings -- flow and fundamentals both deteriorating while the revision signal is not being refreshed (stale, not an early upgrade about to confirm). The Value desk independently rejects it as a trap, so trimming it as a funding source does not collide with any desk establishing a contra long. COFORGE (Q1, flow confirming ARM, mid-cap quality) is the higher-transfer use of that capital.
Risk flags
⚠ CROWDING (LEVEL-agreement): solar-manufacturing (Premier + Waaree + Solar Inds) is one correlated cycle/policy bet entering multiple desks dressed as independent Q1 names -- firm-level cap imposed; CIO to track aggregate solar exposure so sqrt(BR) is not silently shrinking across the firm.⚠ FRAGILITY (force-divergence at scale): VEDL -Rs13,051cr flow vs ARM 70 is an unmarked structural event (de-merger/overhang); firm-wide hold-underweight/do-not-add until flow stabilises -- a single record-scale outflow against a thin-IC revision is a fragility, not a bargain.⚠ CROWDED ANCHOR DEMAND: funds paying up for liquidity/franchise on neutral-weak-ARM index anchors (Maruti +2,559, Hyundai +1,811, Reliance +3,801) -- hold benchmark-weight, do NOT mistake flow-into-anchors for alpha; tilting on it adds beta dressed as edge.⚠ REVISION-ONLY TRAPS (Q2): LICI (ARM 99), ZYDUSLIFE (ARM 91), Bharat Forge (ARM 84) -- highest revisions on the board but funds absent; ARM's ~0.03-0.045 IC is too thin to size alone, so cap these at starters in contra-mandated books only and wait for flow confirmation.⚠ CORRELATED MID-CAP-IT STACKING: Coforge + Hexaware + Mphasis are all the same mid-cap-quality-IT bet -- valid individually but must not stack across Large&Mid / Mid / Flexi / Focused into one factor; effective breadth, not raw name-count, governs sizing.⚠ EARNINGS-QUALITY UNVERIFIED: BHARTIARTL headline PAT -9.8% is held core on flow+ARM but the optical-vs-real PAT delta is NOT independently confirmed from our data -- no desk lifts to overweight in a concentrated book until the earnings caveat is cleared.
Summary
The firm is operating in a stock-picker's tape where sector beta is absent (no float-weight sector ARM above ~63, unweighted median near 50, Industrials weakest at 39.2 FF). The entire edge is Quadrant-1 confluence -- analysts upgrading AND funds buying AND earnings real -- and that breadth is concentrated in Financials (39 Q1), Diversified (37), Consumer (36) and Healthcare (28, the cleanest confirm-breadth). The Fundamental Law governs everything: with per-name ARM IC only ~0.03-0.045 on a 1-6M clock, we harvest by BREADTH of independent confirmed bets and by TRANSFER (sizing them), never by concentration or lone-ARM heroics. The three lenses agree on quality-at-the-large-end (float-weight ARM leading equal-weight in Tech/Consumer/Materials) but DIVERGE loudly on a handful of names -- and those gaps are the real signal. I ruled flow as the leading signal on the two record divergences: VEDL (firm-wide hold-underweight, do-not-add -- a -Rs13,051cr exodus is an unmarked event, not a bargain) and LICI (wait for flow; only a starter in the Value desk's contra mandate). I sanctioned the LT underweight to mandate (Q4 fade, capped by TE, deepest in the thematic Infra book that expresses capex elsewhere). I held BHARTIARTL core on the heaviest fund bid on the board (+Rs8,521cr) + rising ARM but blocked any overweight in concentrated books until the optical-PAT caveat is confirmed. I imposed a firm-level cap on cross-desk solar concentration and confirmed WIPRO as a stale-ARM funding source with no cross-desk conflict. Division stance: overweight Core Equity (the breadth engine, Multicap IR 0.71 leads), selective Thematic (overweight Healthcare/Infra, humble on near-zero-IC Tech/Consumption), neutral Strategy/Hybrid/Quant with defensive optionality (Aggressive Hybrid 72% equity + dry powder) approved on a thin-breadth board.
